Job.JobDescription:
The JV/ASST. COACH - BOYS BASKETBALL position at SOUTHERN GUILFORD HIGH is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The JV/ASST. COACH - BOYS BASKETBALL is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At SOUTHERN GUILFORD HIGH, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The JV/ASST. COACH - BOYS BASKETBALL encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The JV/ASST. COACH - BOYS BASKETBALL also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the JV/ASST. COACH - BOYS BASKETBALL role at SOUTHERN GUILFORD HIGH is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

TITLE: Non-Faculty Coach QUALIFICATIONS: High School Diploma or GED REPORTS TO: Athletic Director/Principal JOB GOAL: Position is responsible for the specific athletic team/program as assigned. Position assists with conducting practices, motivating students, and instructing student athletes in game strategies and techniques. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Assists the head coach with instructing students in the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport. 2. Organizes and directs individual and small group practice activities/exercises as directed by the head coach. 3. Assesses players' skills, monitors students during competition and practice, and keeps the head coach informed of the athletic performance of students. 4. Assists with determining game strategy. 5. Assists the head coach with supervising athletes during practices and competitions. 6. Follows established procedures in the event of an injury. 7. Models sports-like behavior and maintains appropriate conduct towards students, officials, and spectators. 8. Maintains the equipment room in orderly condition and assumes responsibility for its security. 9. Distributes equipment, supplies, and uniforms to students as directed by the head coach. 10. Assists the head coach with submitting a list of award winners at the end of the season. 11. Participates in special activities, including parents' night, banquets, award nights, and pep assemblies. 12. Performs support tasks such as distributing and maintaining eligibility forms, emergency data cards, and other related records. 13. Models nondiscriminatory practices in all activities. Terms of Employment: Duration of Coaching Season Starting Salary and/or Grade: Non-Faculty Coach Stipend Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities: Must have the ability to sit and stand for extended periods of time; exhibit manual dexterity to dial a telephone, see and read a computer screen and printed material with or without vision aids; hear and understand speech at normal levels, outdoors and on the telephone; speak in audible tones so that others may understand clearly; physical agility to lift up to 25 pounds to shoulder height and 50 pounds to waist height; and to bend, to stoop, to sit on the floor, to climb stairs, to walk and to reach overhead. Some experience as a Wrestling coach at the high school or college level preferred. Must possess effective coaching techniques and skills. Must possess some knowledge of the rules, regulations, strategies, and techniques of the sport. Must possess the ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with school administrators, parents, and students.

Job.JobTitle: Assistant Coach - Boys Swimming and Diving
Job.JobDescription:
South High is looking for an Assistant Boys Swimming and Diving Coach to work with divers. The season will run from December 1st - March 27th.
Qualifications: - Candidate understands and promotes the role of education-based athletics within the overall educational experience - Candidate can provide evidence of possessing the ability to positively lead, teach and motivate high school students - Candidate possesses the ability to effectively lead and influence a coaching staff - Candidate possesses the ability to clearly and effectively communicate with a variety of program stakeholders - Candidate possesses a superior knowledge of the fundamentals, strategies, and teaching points associated with the sport swimming and diving - Candidate can articulate a program purpose statement which describes the benefits of participation within the program beyond just the development of athletic skills
Salary: Co-Curricular Salary Schedule: Base salary plus experience and length of season
Application Procedures All applications for open Fargo Public Schools positions must be made via www.applitrack.com/fargo/onlineapp. Paper submissions will not be accepted. Application materials must clearly explain how experience and education are related to the minimum qualifications and job duties. Applicants for administrative, teaching, professional and other certified staff positions will need to attach: 1) Cover Letter 2) Resume/CV 3) Three (3) Letters of Reference 4) Complete transcripts for each degree from the institutions conferring the degrees. In addition, Teacher Applicants will need to attach: Current teaching license.
Applicants who are residents of ND and eligible to claim veteran's preference must upload a Form DD214. Claims for disabled veteran's preference must include a current statement of disabled status from the Veterans Affairs Office.
For more information or questions please call 701-446-1000 or email humanresources@fargo.k12.nd.us.
Job.SalaryRange: Co-Curricular Salary Schedule: Base salary plus experience and length of season

Job.JobDescription:
The Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) role at Lincoln Public Schools NE is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Lincoln Public Schools NE, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Lincoln Public Schools NE, the Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) at Lincoln Public Schools NE is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.
Job.Location: North Star High School - Lincoln, Nebraska

Job.JobDescription:
The JV/ASST. COACH - BASEBALL at Guilford County is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the JV/ASST. COACH - BASEBALL are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Guilford County use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The JV/ASST. COACH - BASEBALL recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the JV/ASST. COACH - BASEBALL collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The JV/ASST. COACH - BASEBALL maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Guilford County. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

COACH - BOYS BASKETBALL at SOUTHERN GUILFORD HIGH is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. The Assistant Football Coach- Offensive Coordinator at Union County Public Schools is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Assistant Football Coach- Offensive Coordinator plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Union County Public Schools reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Assistant Football Coach- Offensive Coordinator expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Assistant Football Coach- Offensive Coordinator supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Assistant Football Coach- Offensive Coordinator may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Union County Public Schools. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Job.JobDescription:
The Head Volleyball Coach at Union County Public Schools is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Head Volleyball Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Union County Public Schools, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Head Volleyball Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Head Volleyball Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Union County Public Schools operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Union County Public Schools with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Head Volleyball Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Head Volleyball Coach role at Union County Public Schools.
Job.CompanyDescription: Union County Public Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
The Assistant Track Coach role at Hillside High School emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Assistant Track Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Hillside High School, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Assistant Track Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Hillside High School at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Assistant Track Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Assistant Track Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Assistant Track Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Hillside High School.

The Head Womens Soccer Coach at Hillside High School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the Head Womens Soccer Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Hillside High School, the Head Womens Soccer Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The Head Womens Soccer Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Hillside High School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The Head Womens Soccer Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

Job.JobTitle: Assistant Coach -Varsity Baseball @ Hunter Huss High
Job.JobDescription:
Gaston County is looking for a Baseball Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Definition An employee in this position will be a personal example of high moral character and superior teaching standards. The Assistant Baseball Coach will teach good sportsmanship as the number one priority on the athletic field. This Coach will work with the Head Football Coach, faculty, and staff to support player academic and moral success.
Duties and Responsibilities
• Follows the lead of the Head Baseball Coach. • Promotes the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not other sports programs. • Promotes the sport outside the school. • Assists with the implementation of the athletic policies by the entire staff of the sports program. • Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans. • Understands the proper administrative line of command and refers all requests or grievances through proper channels. • Maintains discipline, adjust grievances, and work to increase morale and cooperation. • Provides training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete. • Gives constant attention to a student athlete's grades and conduct. • Attends all practices and games. • Provides assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant. • Completes and submits proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries to the county athletic office within 24 hours, if directed by the Head Coach. • Directs student managers, assistants, trainers, and statisticians. • Assists the Head Coach in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for the tournament and special sports events. • Assists in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. • Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees, if directed by the Head Coach. • Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. • Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. • Permits the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. • Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on the general cleanliness of the facility. • Is responsible for the cleanliness and maintenance of specific sports equipment. • Secures all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving the building if custodians are not on duty. • Instills in each player respect for equipment and school property, its care, and proper use. • Performs such other duties as assigned by the Head Coach and/or administration.
Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities
• Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Gaston County Schools' Board of Education. • Knowledge of existing system, state, and conference regulations; implements them consistently and interprets them for staff. • Ability and willingness to organize and assist with the supervision of the baseball program. • Must have superior knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. • Thorough understanding of the growth and development of students/athletes at the assigned school.
Physical Requirements
• Must be able to physically perform the basic life operational functions of climbing, balancing, stooping, kneeling, crouching, crawling, reaching, standing, walking, pushing, lifting, grasping, talking, hearing, and repetitive motions. • Must be able to perform very heavy work exerting in excess of 100 pounds of force occasionally, and/or in excess of 50 pounds of force frequently and/or in excess of 25 pounds of force constantly to lift objects. • Must possess the visual acuity to operate required equipment and motor vehicles, use measuring devices, and inspect work for accuracy, thoroughness, and adherence to work standards and instructions. • Must also possess the visual acuity to prepare data and statistics, work with figures, and records processes, operate a computer, and do extensive research and reading.
Training and Experience
• Graduation from high school or equivalent, preferred. • Previous successful coaching experience in football. • Coaching experience in a public school system, preferred.
This Position Reports Directly to: Head Coach, Athletic Director, and Principal
Classification: Non-exempt
Salary: Gaston County Schools' Coach Supplement Schedule
Disclaimer The preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications required of employees for this job.
If offered a position, the prospective employee must pass a required health exam, criminal records check, and drug test.
Job.SalaryRange: Gaston County Schools' Coach Supplement Schedule

Job.JobDescription:
The Boys Varsity Assistant Basketball Athletic Coach | Paisley Middle School position at Tabetha Mabe is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Boys Varsity Assistant Basketball Athletic Coach | Paisley Middle School works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Boys Varsity Assistant Basketball Athletic Coach | Paisley Middle School at Tabetha Mabe sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Tabetha Mabe, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Boys Varsity Assistant Basketball Athletic Coach | Paisley Middle School encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Boys Varsity Assistant Basketball Athletic Coach | Paisley Middle School is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Boys Varsity Assistant Basketball Athletic Coach | Paisley Middle School position at Tabetha Mabe is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Winston Salem/Forsyth County is looking for a Basketball Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Girls Coach: Varsity Basketball

Job.JobDescription:
Riverwood Middle is looking for a Soccer Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Coach - Girl's Soccer at Riverwood Middle is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Head Coach - Girl's Soccer is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Riverwood Middle, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Head Coach - Girl's Soccer helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Head Coach - Girl's Soccer works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Riverwood Middle operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Riverwood Middle with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Head Coach - Girl's Soccer reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Head Coach - Girl's Soccer role at Riverwood Middle.
Riverwood Middle is looking for a Softball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Riverwood Middle is looking for a Baseball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.

Turrentine Middle is looking for a Wrestling coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Athletic Coach- Wrestling- Turrentine Middle role at Turrentine Middle is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Athletic Coach- Wrestling- Turrentine Middle is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Turrentine Middle are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Athletic Coach- Wrestling- Turrentine Middle listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Athletic Coach- Wrestling- Turrentine Middle encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Athletic Coach- Wrestling- Turrentine Middle follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Turrentine Middle, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Athletic Coach- Wrestling- Turrentine Middle at Turrentine Middle, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

Turrentine Middle is looking for a Baseball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Athletic Coach- Baseball Assistant- Turrentine position at Turrentine Middle is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Athletic Coach- Baseball Assistant- Turrentine is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Turrentine Middle, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Athletic Coach- Baseball Assistant- Turrentine encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Athletic Coach- Baseball Assistant- Turrentine also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Athletic Coach- Baseball Assistant- Turrentine role at Turrentine Middle is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

Davidson County is looking for a Soccer Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Please view the job description Nicole Clausi at nicoleclausi@davidson.k12.nc.us
Head Varsity Coach
Preferred Valid NC Teaching Licensure Serve as liaison between the coaching staff and AD. Has a thorough knowledge of the rules and regulations of the NCHSAA, DCS, and Conference as they pertain to his/her sport, including verifying the eligibility of all staff members and student-athletes. Will provide a final roster to the AD. This includes following all DCS School Board Policies. Understands the proper administrative chain of command and refers all requests or grievances through the proper channels. Is aware of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. Establishes the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by the staff. Delegates specific duties, supervises the implementation, and evaluates all assistants at the end of the season. Maintains discipline, mediates grievances, and works to increase morale. Assists the AD in scheduling and submitting transportation requests through Travel Tracker. Assists in the necessary preparation for scheduled home contests or practices and adheres to facility usage times. Communicates facility needs/repairs to the AD. Provides proper safeguards, including inspection, of all equipment. Monitors the grades and conduct of his/her student-athletes. Provides supervision, guidance, and safeguards for each student-athlete by his/her presence at all practices, and contests, while traveling, and when returning from off-campus events. Directs student managers, assistants, and statisticians. Determines discipline and procedures of due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contacts parents when a student-athlete is suspended, dropped, or becomes ineligible. Participates in the budgeting process with the AD by submitting needs for the next season. Is accountable for all equipment in his/her program and submits notification to the AD for any equipment lost, damaged, or not returned. Arranges for issuing, collecting, securing, reconditioning, and inventory of all equipment. Secures all doors, lights, windows, and locks and stores all equipment before leaving campus. Supervises locker room area before and after practice. Instills in each player respect for equipment and school property in its care and proper use. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans. Responsible for reporting scores to required outlets set forth by the NCHSAA. This duty may be delegated by the head coach. Guarantees that all district equipment including buses is used for official school business only and is operated safely. Performs other duties which may be assigned by the AD or Principal.
Job.Location: West Davidson High - Lexington, North Carolina

Job.Location: Maiden Middle School - Maiden, North Carolina

Job.JobDescription:
Catawba County is looking for a Volleyball Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
ATHLETIC COACH (to include Cheerleading)
Under general supervision, assist the board in the fair and just discharge of its obligation to students and staff through performance of the Head Coach role over and beyond the duties of a classroom teacher. Reports to the school principal and athletic director.
SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Catawba County Schools Board of Education and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the sports program.
Knowledge of existing school system, state, and conference regulations; implements them consistently and interprets them for staff.
Understand the proper administrative line of command and refer all requests of grievances through proper channels. Awareness of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance.
Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by staff. Design conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program.
Train and inform staff, encourage professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy.
Delegate specific duty, supervise implementation, and at season's end, analyze staff effectiveness and evaluate all assistants.
Maintain discipline, adjust grievances, and work to increase morale and cooperation.
Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for tournament and special sport events.
Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times. Coordinate program with maintenance and school employees.
Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility.
Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites.
Advise the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method, or procedural changes.
Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations.
Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records.
Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing.
Monitor equipment rooms and coaches' offices, authorize who may enter, issue or requisition equipment.
Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times.
Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Be responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment.
Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty.
Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings.
Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program. Promote the sport outside the school through news media, community recreation programs, or in any other feasible manner.
Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media.
Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans.
Present information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results.
Perform other duties as assigned by the school Principal in keeping with Catawba County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes.
KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES
Must possess administrative and organizational skills in addition to a thorough knowledge of Catawba County Schools athletic policy and procedure as well as the North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes. Ability to communicate effectively with students, staff, parents, and the community.
DISCLAIMER
The preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications required of employees to this job.
Position Type: Part-Time Job Categories: Athletics & Activities > Coaching
Job Requirements At least 2 year(s) of relevant experience preferred. High School/Trade School degree preferred. Citizenship, residency or work visa required
Job.Experience: 2 years

Job.JobDescription:
Catawba County is looking for a Soccer Head JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
ATHLETIC COACH (to include Cheerleading)
SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Adhere to the athletic policy of the Catawba County Board of Education and the NCHSAA.
Establish and maintain harmonious relations with fellow coaches, faculty, students, and parents.
Attend all athletic department, NCHSAA and conference meetings, as required.
Maintain professional growth through attendance of clinics and other professional activities.
Assist the athletic director in the inventory of equipment and supplies and make recommendations for new or replacement equipment.
Assist the athletic director in the preparation of athletic contest contracts, when requested.
Assist the athletic director in the preparation of athletic eligibility lists, when requested.
Responsible for the issuance, care, and return of athletic equipment, following the Catawba County Schools procedure for lost equipment.
Assist the athletic director in providing necessary information for the preparation of athletic transportation requests.
Assist the athletic director in the development of the athletic budget, when requested.
Assume responsibility for the supervision of athletes and teams before, during, and after practices, contests, and while traveling.
Provide the athletic director and principal with a copy of all team rules and regulations.
Oversee the safety conditions of the facility or area in which assigned sport is conducted at all times that students are present.
Report injuries to the athletic director/trainer and principal.
Adhere to the Catawba County Schools athletic training and medical procedures.
Delegate specific duty, supervise implementation, and at season’s end, analyze staff effectiveness and evaluate all assistants.
Maintain discipline, adjust grievances, and work to increase morale and cooperation.
Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for tournament and special sport events.
Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times. Coordinate program with maintenance and school employees.
Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility.
Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Advise the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method or procedural changes.
Provide training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant.
Give constant attention to a student athlete’s grades and conduct.
Presence at all practices and games. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant.
Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies.
Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries to athletic office within 24 hours.
Direct student managers, assistants, and statisticians.
Determine discipline, delineate procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contact parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible.
Assist athletes in their college or advanced educational selection.
Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations.
Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records.
KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES
Thorough knowledge of all aspects of the sport/activity being coached
Thorough knowledge of the rules and regulations concerning the sport/activity being coached
Considerable knowledge of the NCHSAA rules and regulations
MINIMUM TRAINING AND EXPERIENCE
Four-year degree preferred, but not required
Prior coaching experience preferred
Must meet all NCHSAA coaching requirements
Experience in assigned sport.

It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications required of employees to this job.

Job.JobDescription:
Thomasville Middle School is looking for a Basketball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The 2025-2026 TMS Assistant Men's Basketball Coach role at Thomasville Middle School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the 2025-2026 TMS Assistant Men's Basketball Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Thomasville Middle School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The 2025-2026 TMS Assistant Men's Basketball Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the 2025-2026 TMS Assistant Men's Basketball Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The 2025-2026 TMS Assistant Men's Basketball Coach follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Thomasville Middle School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the 2025-2026 TMS Assistant Men's Basketball Coach at Thomasville Middle School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.
Thomasville High School is looking for a Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job.Location: Thomasville High School - Thomasville, North Carolina

Job.JobDescription:
SUMMARY:
The job of Head Football Coach was established for the purpose to assist the board in the fair and just discharge its obligation to students and staff through performance of the Head Coach role over and beyond the duties of a classroom teacher.
RESPONSIBILITIES & DUTIES:
* Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Caswell County Schools Board of Education and is responsible for their implementation by the entire staff of the sports program * Knowledge of existing system, state and conference regulations; implements same consistently and interprets them for his/her staff * Understands the proper administrative line of command and refers all requests or grievances through proper channels. Is aware of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance * Maintains discipline, resolves grievances, and works to increase morale and cooperation * Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries to athletic office within 24 hours * Direct student managers, assistants and statisticians * Determine discipline, delineate procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contact parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible * Assist athletes in the college or advanced educational selection * Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations * Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records * Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing * Monitor equipment rooms and coaches' offices, authorize who may enter, issue or requisition equipment * Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times * Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Be responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment * Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty * Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use * Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings * Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program. Promote the sport outside the school through news media, community recreation programs, or in any other feasible manner * Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media * Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans * Present information to the news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results. Perform other duties as assigned by the school Principal in keeping with Caswell County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes
MINIMUM EDUCATION, EXPERIENCE AND REQUIREMENTS:
* As applicable, must meet requirements established by the North Carolina High School Athletic Association, North Carolina Department of Public Instruction, the Caswell County Board of Education, and school administration * Teacher license and employment as a teacher by the Caswell County Board of Education is strongly preferred * Applicant may be asked to hold a 10-month teaching position, plus one additional month for coaching duties * Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport
KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:
* Must possess administrative and organizational skills in addition to a thorough knowledge of Caswell County Schools athletic policy and procedure as well as the North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes
PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS:
While performing the duties of this job, the employee is frequently required to stand, walk, sit, talk and listen. The employee is occasionally required to use hands to fingers, handle, or feel; reach with hands and arms; and stoop, kneel, crouch or crawl. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 50 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus. The employee is regularly exposed to outside weather conditions.
Job.SalaryRange: Based on State and Local Salary Schedule

Job.JobDescription:
Swain Middle School is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Middle School Athletic Coaches position at Swain Middle School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Middle School Athletic Coaches works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Middle School Athletic Coaches at Swain Middle School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Swain Middle School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Middle School Athletic Coaches encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Middle School Athletic Coaches is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Middle School Athletic Coaches position at Swain Middle School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
Job.Location: Swain Middle School - Bryson City, North Carolina

Job.JobDescription:
Swain Middle School is looking for a Baseball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The MS Baseball Head Coach position at Swain Middle School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the MS Baseball Head Coach prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Swain Middle School, the MS Baseball Head Coach works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The MS Baseball Head Coach encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the MS Baseball Head Coach at Swain Middle School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.
Job.Location: Swain Middle School - Bryson City, North Carolina

Job.JobDescription:
Edenton-Chowan is looking for a Track & Field coach to join our program. Apply now.
Chowan Middle School is seeking a dedicated and enthusiastic individual to join our athletic department as a Middle School Track Coach for the upcoming season. The ideal candidate will possess a passion for running and a commitment to fostering a positive and inclusive environment for student-athletes. This seasonal position involves coaching and mentoring middle school students, promoting physical fitness, teamwork, and a love for the sport of Track.
Responsibilities - Plan and conduct daily practice sessions that focus on skill development, physical conditioning, and teamwork. - Develop and implement training programs tailored to the varying skill levels of student-athletes. - Organize and oversee Track meets, including transportation, logistics, and coordination with other schools. - Foster a positive team culture that emphasizes sportsmanship, respect, and personal growth.

Job.JobTitle: High School Head Boys' Tennis Coach (2026-27)
Job.JobDescription: JOB TITLE: Head Boys' Tennis Coach - Bennington High School REPORTS TO: Activities Director TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: Part-Time I. ACCOUNTABILITY OBJECTIVES: To instruct athletes in fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. The student shall also receive instruction that will end to the formation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, self-confidence, resilience, and cooperation with teammates. Regular, dependable, in-person attendance is an essential function of the job. II. POSITION CHARACTERISTICS: Recruited by: Athletic Director/Principal Wages: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Paid Vacation: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Paid Holidays: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Sick Leave: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Work Day: Hours to be determined by Activities Director/Principal Pay Day: As determined by Negotiated Agreement. III. POSITION RELATIONSHIPS: Reports to Activities Director Coordinates with: Activities Director, Building Principal, Custodians, and Transportation Supervisor. IV. POSITION RESPONSIBILITIES: Be responsible for the development and formulation of his/her program in grades 9-12 Oversee a systematic skill development program for all athletes in the program Recommend assistant coaches and coach's aides and officials to the Activities Director Essential Functions The essential functions of the position include: 1. regular, dependable attendance on the job; 2. the ability to perform the identified tasks and to possess and utilize the identified knowledge, skills and abilities to perform the identified work activities; and, 3. The ability to perform the identified physical requirements as outlined in working environment. Working Environment The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is required to lift and pull or push articles up to 50 pounds. He/she sits most of the time but must walk frequently and, at times, stand. The employee is required to reach with his/her hands and continuously to repeat the same hand, arm, or finger motion many times as in typing. Manual and finger dexterity are essential to the job. He/she is constantly working to meet deadlines with severe time restraints. He/she must see and read fine print such as typed or handwritten materials and have good depth perception (fine muscle control). The employee must, also, be able to hear conversation in a noisy environment and to communicate clearly and concisely through speech. The information contained in this job description is for compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and is not an exhaustive list of the duties performed for this position. Additional duties are performed by the individuals currently holding this position and additional duties may be assigned. EOE Position Type: Part-Time Job Categories: Athletics & Activities > Coaching

Gorham Public Schools is looking for a Lacrosse coach to join our program. Apply now.
GORHAM SCHOOL DEPARTMENT The Gorham School Department has the following coaching position available: 1 st Team Boys Lacrosse Coach If interested, please submit cover letter, resume and completed application by going to http://gorhamschools.org/, click on Human Resources, Employment Opportunities, follow the application link. CHRC required. Applications close when a suitable candidate has been selected. EOE

MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: 1. High school graduate or equivalent. 2. Applicants must have experience in high school level coaching or playing at college or university level in sport to which they are applying. 3. Applicant must have a time schedule compatible with that of the high school. 4. CPR and/or first aide training preferred. 5. Demonstrated ability to work with parents and children.
REPORTS TO: Assistant Principal of Activities and Athletics RATE OF PAY: According to the Master Agreement - Schedule "B" BEGINNING DATE: 2026/27 Fall Season
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. The head coach will be responsible for the entire organization of the sport to which he/she is assigned. 2. The head coach will be directly responsible for all equipment in his/her sport. A complete inventory of all equipment will be filed with the director at the end of each season. 3. The head coach will be responsible for all awards presented in his/her sport and a season summary. 4. Bending, lifting, and overhead work required. 5. Performs other tasks as assigned. This description is intended to describe the essential job functions, the general supplemental functions and the essential requirements for the performance of this job. It is not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities and requirements of a person so classified. Other functions may be assigned and management retains the right to add or change the duties at any time.
EVALUATION: Performance of this position will be evaluated annually.
METHOD OF APPLICATION: Internal and external applicants should complete the online application in the Frontline system. External applicants will be hired and paid by EDUStaff. www.EDUStaff.org
DEADLINE FOR APPLICATION: Open until filled POSTED: 01/08/26
