Physical Demands
While performing the essential duties of this position, the employee is regularly required to stand, walk, talk, and hear. The position frequently requires physical demonstration of skills, including running, jumping, grappling techniques, and general body movement. The employee may be required to lift and carry items weighing more than 20 pounds. Occasional prolonged sitting and frequent travel by bus are required. Extended hours may be necessary to meet the demands of the position.
Work Environment
The work environment noise level is usually moderate but may occasionally be excessive. Work is performed both indoors and outdoors and may involve exposure to varying weather conditions.
Reasonable accommodations will be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions of the position.

The 25-ATH-38 Girls JVB Head Tennis Coach role at North Farmington High School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the 25-ATH-38 Girls JVB Head Tennis Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at North Farmington High School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The 25-ATH-38 Girls JVB Head Tennis Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the 25-ATH-38 Girls JVB Head Tennis Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The 25-ATH-38 Girls JVB Head Tennis Coach follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At North Farmington High School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the 25-ATH-38 Girls JVB Head Tennis Coach at North Farmington High School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

North Farmington High School is looking for a Lacrosse Head JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The 25-ATH-30 Boys JV Head Lacrosse Coach role at North Farmington High School is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the 25-ATH-30 Boys JV Head Lacrosse Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At North Farmington High School, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The 25-ATH-30 Boys JV Head Lacrosse Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At North Farmington High School, the 25-ATH-30 Boys JV Head Lacrosse Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the 25-ATH-30 Boys JV Head Lacrosse Coach at North Farmington High School is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

The listing is not all-inclusive. Qualifications Previous soccer coaching experience with a preference towards previous head coaching experience Superior technical knowledge of the sport of soccer Proven ability to handle administrative responsibilities of a high school athletic program Ability to communicate, foster and maintain positive relationships with students, other coaches, administrators, news media, boosters, and parents Transparent and proactive communication, well organized, and detail oriented Has high expectations and coaches with passion and integrity Successful experience in mentoring and developing a coaching staff Familiarity with local and regional club soccer organizations and willingness to make connections and establish relationships that enables a smooth and positive transition from one season to the next Proven ability to attract students to their program and retain them for multiple years Ability to deal with and resolve conflict Desire to serve others Ability to understand and follow policies (PNHS, PPS, MHSAA) Must undergo a background check and fingerprinting The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. Questions, please contact James Swinehart, athletic director at jswinehart@portageps.org. Job.JobDescription:
The Assistant High School Tennis Coach position at High School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Assistant High School Tennis Coach prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At High School, the Assistant High School Tennis Coach works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Assistant High School Tennis Coach encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Assistant High School Tennis Coach at High School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

The High School Assistant Volleyball Coach at High School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the High School Assistant Volleyball Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At High School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The High School Assistant Volleyball Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The High School Assistant Volleyball Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at High School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent High School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the High School Assistant Volleyball Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the High School Assistant Volleyball Coach role at High School.

The COACH - JV/ASST. COACH - BOYS BASKETBALL at SOUTHWEST GUILFORD MIDDLE is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the COACH - JV/ASST. COACH - BOYS BASKETBALL plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at SOUTHWEST GUILFORD MIDDLE reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The COACH - JV/ASST. COACH - BOYS BASKETBALL expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the COACH - JV/ASST. COACH - BOYS BASKETBALL supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the COACH - JV/ASST. COACH - BOYS BASKETBALL may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at SOUTHWEST GUILFORD MIDDLE. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Bandys High School - Catawba is looking for a Golf Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Reports To: Athletic Director Purpose of Position: To carry out the aims and objectives of the sport program as outlined by the head coach and school administration. To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success.
SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:
Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policy approved by the Catawba County Schools Board of Education and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. Has knowledge of the existing system, state and league regulations; implements same consistently and interprets them for staff. Understands the proper administrative line of command and refers all requests or grievances through proper channels. Is aware of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. Maintains discipline, adjusts grievances, and work to increase morale and cooperation. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for tournament and special sport events. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times. Coordinate program with maintenance and school employees. Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility. Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Advise the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method or procedural changes. Provide training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Give constant attention to a student athlete's grades and conduct. Presence at all practices and games. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant. Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies. Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries to athletic office within 24 hours. Direct student managers, assistants, and statisticians. Determine discipline, delineate procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contact parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. Assist athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations. Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records. Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Be responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty. Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings. Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program. Promote the sport outside the school through news media, community recreation programs, or in any other feasible manner. Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans. Present information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results. Perform other duties as assigned by the school Principal in keeping with Catawba County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes.
KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES Must possess administrative and organizational skills in addition to a thorough knowledge of Catawba County Schools athletic policy and procedure as well as the North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes. Ability to communicate effectively with students, staff, parents, and the community.
MINIMUM TRAINING AND EXPERIENCE As applicable, must meet requirements established by the North Carolina High School Athletic Association, North Carolina Department of Public Instruction, the Catawba County Board of Education, and school administration. Teacher license and employment as a teacher by the Catawba County Board of Education is strongly preferred. Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport.
DISCLAIMER The preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications required of employees to this job.
Job.Location: Bandys High School - Catawba, North Carolina

Job.JobDescription:
The FOOTBALL COACH – HEAD JV COACH at SALEM HIGH SCHOOL is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the FOOTBALL COACH – HEAD JV COACH are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at SALEM HIGH SCHOOL use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The FOOTBALL COACH – HEAD JV COACH recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the FOOTBALL COACH – HEAD JV COACH collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The FOOTBALL COACH – HEAD JV COACH maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at SALEM HIGH SCHOOL. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

The 25-ATH-39 Girls Freshman Head Volleyball Coach at North Farmington High School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the 25-ATH-39 Girls Freshman Head Volleyball Coach manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At North Farmington High School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The 25-ATH-39 Girls Freshman Head Volleyball Coach demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The 25-ATH-39 Girls Freshman Head Volleyball Coach follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the 25-ATH-39 Girls Freshman Head Volleyball Coach at North Farmington High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

The 25-ATH-40 Girls JV Head Volleyball Coach at North Farmington High School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the 25-ATH-40 Girls JV Head Volleyball Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At North Farmington High School, the 25-ATH-40 Girls JV Head Volleyball Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The 25-ATH-40 Girls JV Head Volleyball Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents North Farmington High School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The 25-ATH-40 Girls JV Head Volleyball Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

The 25-ATH-41 Boys JV Head Soccer Coach position at North Farmington High School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the 25-ATH-41 Boys JV Head Soccer Coach prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At North Farmington High School, the 25-ATH-41 Boys JV Head Soccer Coach works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The 25-ATH-41 Boys JV Head Soccer Coach encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the 25-ATH-41 Boys JV Head Soccer Coach at North Farmington High School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

Job.JobTitle: EXTRACURR - COACH - TRACK ASST (Assistant Track and Field Coach at Bozeman High School - Spring, 2026)
Job.JobDescription:
The EXTRACURR - COACH - TRACK ASST position at Bozeman School District #7 is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the EXTRACURR - COACH - TRACK ASST works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The EXTRACURR - COACH - TRACK ASST at Bozeman School District #7 sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Bozeman School District #7, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The EXTRACURR - COACH - TRACK ASST encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The EXTRACURR - COACH - TRACK ASST is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the EXTRACURR - COACH - TRACK ASST position at Bozeman School District #7 is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
Job.Location: Bozeman High School, 205 N 11th Ave, Bozeman, MT 59715

Job.JobDescription:
I. NATURE OF POSITION: A. The High School Coach shall be directly responsible to the building principal, but will work through the Activities Director in carrying out his/her assignments. B. Job.Experience: Successful high school and/or college baseball experience, or other relevant experience.

Job.JobDescription:
Asheville City Schools Job Description
Job Title: Head Girls' Cross Country Coach Location: Asheville High School Contact: Sonita.Warren-Dixon@acsgmail.net
Job Summary: We are seeking a dedicated and knowledgeable Head Girls' Cross Country Coach to lead and develop our high school tennis program. The ideal candidate will have a passion for the sport, experience in coaching or collegiate track and field experience, and the ability to mentor student-athletes while promoting teamwork, sportsmanship, and personal growth.
Key Responsibilities: Coaching & Training: Plan and conduct effective practices to develop players' skills, techniques, and meet strategies. Game Management: Prepare the team for competitions, make strategic decisions, and ensure players compete at their highest level. Player Development: Mentor athletes to improve their abilities and foster sportsmanship, discipline, and teamwork. Safety & Compliance: Ensure adherence to all school and state athletic association regulations regarding safety, eligibility, and conduct. Communication: Maintain clear communication with athletes, parents, school administration, and athletic staff regarding schedules, expectations, and team progress. Academic Support: Encourage student-athletes to maintain academic eligibility and support their success in the classroom. Team Building: Foster a positive, inclusive, and competitive team environment.
Qualifications: Experience: Prior coaching experience (preferred) or a strong background in competitive track. Certifications: CPR/First Aid certification and any required state coaching certifications. Skills: Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills with the ability to motivate and develop student-athletes. Availability: Ability to commit to the season, including practices, meets, and postseason meets.
Preferred Qualifications: Experience coaching at the high school or collegiate level. Knowledge of high school track and field rules and regulations. Ability to drive an activity bus and obtain necessary transportation certifications.
Salary: Based on experience and school district policies.
Job.SalaryRange: Based on experience and school district policies

Job.JobDescription:
Position Title: Head Coach - Football Location: West Fargo High School Department: Athletics/Activities Reports To: Athletic Director Term: Fall 2026 Season Schedule: See Summary for more information. Salary: Position follows the Activities Pay Schedule 25-27 Date of last review: February 2026
SUMMARY: The Head Coach - Football position is responsible for leading the team in training, instruction, conditioning, and performance. The position provides support and education to athletes in an effort to excel in their given sport in a healthy and safe environment. The position views winning not only as success, but as helping team members reach goals.
Schedule: The schedule and hours for this position vary and aligns to the NDHSAA schedule. For questions regarding the schedule, please contact the building-level athletic director.
To Apply: Please complete the application and include three references.
QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS: To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty and requirement satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skills, and/or abilities required for the role. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: (Other duties may be assigned) Collaboration: Leads training, instruction, conditioning, performance, and selection of athletes. Communication: Communicates with other coaches, assistant coaches, and athletes in a professional and respectful manner. Communicates with athletes to provide helpful, positive guidance to ensure athlete growth and safety. Communicates with officials, opposing coaches, parents, and others in a positive, respectful manner. Providing exemplary behavior for athletes and other learners. Compassion: Demonstrates and generates, among staff and athletes, an attitude of good sportsmanship and fair play by adhering to the “Coach’s Code of Ethics” and our conference “Sportsmanship Guidelines.” Understands the complexities of cultural and global issues and how they relate to employee and learners’ experiences. Approaches coaching with a positive, committed mindset. Focusing on athlete well-being. Develops positive working relationships with school administration, learners, educators, and the wider district community. Creativity: Connects with team members and shares ideas in an effort to improve the overall learner and employee experience. Critical Thinking: Develops, organizes, and implements effective practices for athletes to teach proper skills and strategies to compete at an elevated level. Reflection: Maintains communication with supervisor and director regarding general feedback, career advancement opportunities, and areas of growth opportunities. Resilience: Maintains composure while dealing with stressful situations. Proactively seeks out resolutions and takes initiative to resolve knowledge gaps. Responsibility: Adheres to all West Fargo Public Schools and NDHSAA guidelines and policies. Provides for the health, safety, and well-being of team members at practices, games, and transportation to and from activities.
PHYSICAL DEMANDS AND WORK ENVIRONMENT: The physical demands and work environment described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the essential duties and responsibilities of this role, the employee is regularly required to reach with hands and arms. While performing the essential duties and responsibilities of this role, the employee is regularly required to stand, walk, bend, talk, and hear. The specific vision abilities required for this role include close and peripheral vision. The employee may frequently lift up to ~25 pounds without assistance. The employee may occasionally lift up to ~50 pounds without assistance. The employee may have to push/pull up to ~120 pounds while performing the duties of the role. The employee must work with the public and various WFPS staff while simultaneously managing several competing demands. The employee may work outdoors and, if so, will be exposed to the applicable weather elements. The employee may come in contact with bloodborne pathogens or other bodily fluids on rare occasions. The employee will work in an environment that has a quiet to loud noise level.
Education and/or Experience: Currently possesses, or has the ability to obtain, an NDHSAA coaching certification. Experience in the discipline is preferred. Previous experience coaching is preferred.
Language Skills: Ability to read, write, and comprehend simple instructions and short correspondences in the English language. Ability to communicate effectively verbally, expressively, and reactively. Ability to understand and communicate as a subject matter expert for given sport/activity.
Other Skills and Abilities: Exercise confidentiality, discretion, and good judgement. Adhere to the assigned work schedule by maintaining regular and punctual attendance.
SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITIES: None (May supervise other coaches or learners if assigned).
--- The West Fargo Public School District is committed to maintaining a learning and working environment free from discrimination and harassment in all employment and educational programs, activities, and facilities. The District prohibits discrimination and harassment based on an individual's race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, national origin, ancestry, disability, age, or other protected status by law. External applicants are eligible for ND Veteran's Preference: North Dakota veterans claiming preference must submit all proof of eligibility by the closing date. Proof of eligibility includes a form DD214, and if claiming disabled veteran's status, a current letter of disability. West Fargo Public Schools is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusivity in all hiring and employment practices. Applicants who incorporate a holistic perspective and a commitment to inclusion are encouraged to apply.
Job.Experience: Experience in the discipline is preferred. Previous experience coaching is preferred.
Job.SalaryRange: Position follows the Activities Pay Schedule 25-27

Job.JobDescription:
The High School-Girls Assistant Soccer Coach at Robert Conley is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the High School-Girls Assistant Soccer Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Robert Conley reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The High School-Girls Assistant Soccer Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the High School-Girls Assistant Soccer Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the High School-Girls Assistant Soccer Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Robert Conley. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Job.JobDescription:
Holt High School is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Coach-Track (0.75 FTE) (Internal Only) at Holt High School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Assistant Coach-Track (0.75 FTE) (Internal Only) is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Holt High School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Assistant Coach-Track (0.75 FTE) (Internal Only) helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Assistant Coach-Track (0.75 FTE) (Internal Only) works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Holt High School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Holt High School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Assistant Coach-Track (0.75 FTE) (Internal Only) reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Assistant Coach-Track (0.75 FTE) (Internal Only) role at Holt High School.

Job.JobTitle: JV Cheer Sponsor - Fall and Winter for 2026-2027
Job.JobDescription:
The JV Cheer Sponsor - Fall and Winter for 2026 role at Holt High School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the JV Cheer Sponsor - Fall and Winter for 2026 is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Holt High School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The JV Cheer Sponsor - Fall and Winter for 2026 listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the JV Cheer Sponsor - Fall and Winter for 2026 encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The JV Cheer Sponsor - Fall and Winter for 2026 follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Holt High School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the JV Cheer Sponsor - Fall and Winter for 2026 at Holt High School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.
Job.SalaryRange: starts at step 1 $2,338.00 each, for both fall and winter seasons

Job.JobTitle: Fall and Winter Cheer Sponsor (9th Grade) for 2026-2027
Job.JobDescription:
The Fall and Winter Cheer Sponsor (9th Grade) for 2026 role at Holt High School is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Fall and Winter Cheer Sponsor (9th Grade) for 2026 is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Holt High School, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Fall and Winter Cheer Sponsor (9th Grade) for 2026 sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Holt High School, the Fall and Winter Cheer Sponsor (9th Grade) for 2026 is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Fall and Winter Cheer Sponsor (9th Grade) for 2026 at Holt High School is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.
Job.SalaryRange: starts at step 1 $1,870.00 each, for both fall and winter seasons

JOB DESCRIPTION GENERAL STATEMENT OF JOB The Strength & Conditioning Coach is responsible for designing, implementing, and supervising safe and effective strength, conditioning, and injury-prevention programs for student-athletes. This role supports athletic performance, physical development, and overall student wellness while aligning with school, district, and state athletic guidelines. QUALIFICATIONS NC State Teachers Salary Schedule (Based on degree and experience) AND/OR Local Athletic Supplement Strength & Conditioning Coach $739.20 - $1593.90 per season Plus Local Teacher Supplement QUALIFICATIONS
Bachelor’s degree in Exercise Science, Kinesiology, Physical Education, or a related field preferred. Certification from a nationally recognized strength and conditioning organization (e.g., NSCA-CSCS, CSCCA-SCCC) preferred. CPR/AED and First Aid certification required. Previous experience as a strength and conditioning coach at the high school or collegiate level preferred. Knowledge of age-appropriate training methodologies and long-term athletic development principles. Strong communication, organizational, and interpersonal skills. Ability to work effectively with students, coaches, and administrators.
ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS
Design and implement year-round strength and conditioning programs for all athletic teams, in collaboration with head coaches. Supervise and instruct student-athletes during training sessions, ensuring proper technique and safety. Conduct regular fitness assessments to monitor progress and adjust programs as needed. Develop and implement injury-prevention protocols and work with athletic trainers on rehabilitation programs. Educate student-athletes on nutrition, hydration, and other wellness topics. Maintain the cleanliness and organization of the weight room and other training facilities. Manage and maintain strength and conditioning equipment, reporting any issues or needs. Stay current with research and best practices in strength and conditioning. Collaborate with coaches to integrate strength and conditioning into overall team training plans. Foster a positive and motivating training environment. Adhere to all school, district, and state athletic policies and regulations. Communicate effectively with student-athletes, parents, coaches, and administrators. Perform other duties as assigned by the Athletic Director or Principal.
Job.Location: Pasquotank County High School - Elizabeth City, North Carolina

Job.CompanyName: Pasquotank County High School - Elizabeth City

Job.JobDescription:
Northeast High School is looking for a Tennis coach to join our program. Apply now.
Northeast is seeking a coach to provide leadership for the reserve girls tennis program. Good communication and organizational skills are required as well as the ability to develop positive relationships with student-athletes, parents, and the community. Must have a comprehensive knowledge of the sport. This position will require acting as the head coach at Reserve level matches, and to help train players to progress through the levels of high school tennis.
Requires certified teacher or coach who holds a Nebraska Department of Education Teacher or Administrative Certificate or a Nebraska Department of Education Special Services.
This position also require NFHS Certifications in Concussion Awareness, Sudden Cardiac Arrest, and Heat Illness. Also this position requires CPR Certification. These certifications may be completed after the hiring is confirmed.
This position is subject to a veterans preference.
Job Description: Coach

Northwest High School is looking for a Soccer coach to join our program. Apply now.
Northwest High School Reserve Boys Soccer Coach LPS assistant coaches are expected to be organized, hard-working, and fundamentally sound in the sport of soccer, as well as have the desire and ability to develop positive relationships with student-participants, families, and the LPS community. Must have comprehensive knowledge and experience with the sport of soccer to help develop the school’s program.
LPS assistant coaches are expected to follow the guidance of their head coaches in organizing and leading practices, regular season and district contests and performances (not to exceed NSAA/LPS allowances); facilitate, manage and provide oversight of the program; maintain required certification and requirements of the position; and lead their teams in NSAA state championship preparation and performances if the team or individuals qualify.
LPS has a long tradition of academic, athletic and activity excellence. Belief in the value of educational-based athletics is critical.
Requires teacher and/or coach who holds a Nebraska Department of Education Teacher or Administrative Certificate or a Nebraska Department of Education Special Services Coaching Permit.
Knowledge and understanding of NSAA and LPS policies and bylaws is required.
This position is subject to a veterans preference.
Job Description: Coach

Job.JobTitle: High School Assistant Track and Field Coach (2026-2027)
Job.JobDescription:
BENNINGTON PUBLIC SCHOOLS Position Description Position Title: Assistant High School Track and Field Coach (2026-2027)
JOB DESCRIPTION
JOB TITLE: Assistant High School Track and Field Coach - Bennington High School REPORTS TO: Activities Director TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: Part-Time
I. ACCOUNTABILITY OBJECTIVES: To instruct athletes in fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. The student shall also receive instruction that will end to the formation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, self-confidence, resilience, and cooperation with teammates. Regular, dependable, in-person attendance is an essential function of the job.
II. POSITION CHARACTERISTICS: Recruited by: Athletic Director/Principal Wages: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Paid Vacation: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Paid Holidays: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Sick Leave: As determined by Negotiated Agreement III. POSITION RELATIONSHIPS: Reports to Activities Director Coordinates with: Activities Director, Building Principal, Custodians, and Transportation Supervisor. IV. POSITION RESPONSIBILITIES: Be responsible for the development and formulation of his/her program in grades 9-12 Oversee a systematic skill development program for all athletes in the program Recommend assistant coaches and coach's aides and officials to the Activities Director
Essential Functions The essential functions of the position include: 1. regular, dependable attendance on the job; 2. the ability to perform the identified tasks and to possess and utilize the identified knowledge, skills and abilities to perform the identified work activities; and, 3. the ability to perform the identified physical requirements as outlined in working environment.
Working Environment The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is required to lift and pull or push articles up to 50 pounds. He/she sits most of the time but must walk frequently and, at times, stand. The employee is required to reach with his/her hands and continuously to repeat the same hand, arm, or finger motion many times as in typing. Manual and finger dexterity are essential to the job. He/she is constantly working to meet deadlines with severe time restraints. He/she must see and read fine print such as typed or handwritten materials and have good depth perception (fine muscle control). The employee must, also, be able to hear conversation in a noisy environment and to communicate clearly and concisely through speech. The information contained in this job description is for compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and is not an exhaustive list of the duties performed for this position. Additional duties are performed by the individuals currently holding this position and additional duties may be assigned.
EOE
Position Type: Part-Time
Contact Information Human Resources 11620 North 156th Street Bennington, Nebraska 68007 Phone: 402-238-3044 Email: hr@bennps.org

SALEM HIGH SCHOOL is looking for a Football Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION: FOOTBALL COACH � FRESHMAN HEAD COACH Salem High School MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: 1. High school graduate or equivalent. 2. Applicants must have experience in high school level coaching or playing at college or university level in sport to which they are applying. 3. Applicant must have a time schedule compatible with that of the high school. 4. CPR and/or first aide training preferred. 5. Demonstrated ability to work with parents and children. Coach will be responsible for the Freshman team. 2. Coach will be directly responsible for all equipment. A complete inventory of all equipment will be filed with the athletic director at the end of each season. 3. Coach will be responsible for all awards presented in sport and a season summary. 4. Requires off-season time commitment, including staff meetings, conditioning, fundraisers and summer camps. 5. Bending, lifting, and overhead work required. 6. Performs other tasks as assigned. It is not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities and requirements of a person so classified. Other functions may be assigned and management retains the right to add or change the duties at any time. EVALUATION: Performance of this position will be evaluated annually. METHOD OF APPLICATION: Internal and external applicants should complete the online application in the Frontline system. External applicants will be hired and paid by EDUStaff. www.EDUStaff.org DEADLINE FOR APPLICATION: Open until filled Tim Baechler Assistant Principal of Activities and Athletics Salem High School

Job.JobDescription:
The Middle School Sideline Cheer Coach position at Smith Middle School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Middle School Sideline Cheer Coach works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Middle School Sideline Cheer Coach at Smith Middle School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Smith Middle School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Middle School Sideline Cheer Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Middle School Sideline Cheer Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Middle School Sideline Cheer Coach position at Smith Middle School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Finally, the Varsity Assistant Cross Country Coach (2 Positions) position at Troy High School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The Varsity Assistant Cross Country Coach role at Troy High School emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Varsity Assistant Cross Country Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Troy High School, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Varsity Assistant Cross Country Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Troy High School at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Varsity Assistant Cross Country Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Varsity Assistant Cross Country Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Varsity Assistant Cross Country Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Troy High School.

Job.JobTitle: EXTRACURR - COACH - ASST BASEBALL (Assistant Baseball Coach at Bozeman High School)
Job.JobDescription:
The EXTRACURR - COACH - ASST BASEBALL (Assistant Baseball Coach at Bozeman High School) at Bozeman High Sch. Dist. is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the EXTRACURR - COACH - ASST BASEBALL (Assistant Baseball Coach at Bozeman High School) are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Bozeman High Sch. Dist. use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The EXTRACURR - COACH - ASST BASEBALL (Assistant Baseball Coach at Bozeman High School) recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the EXTRACURR - COACH - ASST BASEBALL (Assistant Baseball Coach at Bozeman High School) collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The EXTRACURR - COACH - ASST BASEBALL (Assistant Baseball Coach at Bozeman High School) maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Bozeman High Sch. Dist.. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.
