JOB TITLE: Head Football Coach REPORTS TO: Athletic Director and Principal SALARY: Based on State and Local Salary Schedule SUMMARY: The job of Head Football Coach was established for the purpose to assist the board in the fair and just discharge its obligation to students and staff through performance of the Head Coach role over and beyond the duties of a classroom teacher. RESPONSIBILITIES & DUTIES: Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Caswell County Schools Board of Education and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the sports program Knowledge of existing school system, state, and conference regulations; implements them consistently and interprets them for staff. Understand the proper administrative line of command and refer all requests of grievances through proper channels. Awareness of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by staff. Design conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program Train and inform staff, encourage professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy Delegate specific duty, supervise implementation, and at season’s end, analyze staff effectiveness and evaluate all assistants Maintain discipline, adjust grievances, and work to increase cooperation and morale Coordinate program with maintenance and school employees. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites Advise the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method or procedural changes Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings Provide training rules and any other unique regulations o the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, it’s care and proper use Give constant attention to a student athlete’s grades and conduct Presence at all practices and games. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries to athletic office within 24 hours Direct student managers, assistants and statisticians Determine discipline, delineate procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contact parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible Assist athletes in the college or advanced educational selection Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices, authorize who may enter, issue or requisition equipment Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Be responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program. Promote the sport outside the school through news media, community recreation programs, or in any other feasible manner Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans Present information to the news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results. Perform other duties as assigned by the school Principal in keeping with Caswell County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes MINIMUM EDUCATION, EXPERIENCE AND REQUIREMENTS: As applicable, must meet requirements established by the North Carolina High School Athletic Association, North Carolina Department of Public Instruction, the Caswell County Board of Education, and school administration Teacher license and employment as a teacher by the Caswell County Board of Education is strongly preferred Applicant may be asked to hold a 10-month teaching position, plus one additional month for coaching duties Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES : Must possess administrative and organizational skills in addition to a thorough knowledge of Caswell County Schools athletic policy and procedure as well as the North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS: While performing the duties of this job, the employee is frequently required to stand, walk, sit, talk and listen. The employee is occasionally required to use hands to fingers, handle, or feel: reach with hands and arms; and stoop, kneel, crouch or crawl. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 15 pounds or more at times.

Athletic Coach The coach should understand his/her sport and be familiar with all rules, courtesies, and procedures of the activity. The coach should know his/her students and be an effective leader. He/she should be concerned with winning gracefully and, when losing, accept the loss with poise and a realization that an educational experience probably has been achieved. Specific Duties Teach the physical skills needed to play specific sport. Know and follow all rules and regulations governing the s Provide fair and equal opportunities for all candidate Plan and supervise practice sessions in keeping with the age and maturity of the play Meet with athletic director to prepare a tentative schedule of game Establish rules for the proper use and care of athletic equipmen Prepare and present to the athletic director a list of students desiring to participate so that eligibility can be determ Check and monitor team members' academic Maintain an inventory of all equipment and supplies. Submit to the athletic director at the end of the season a list of any equipment and/or uniforms needed for the upcoming y Submit a budget request to athletic director Collect uniforms and equipment at the end of the sport season and account for same. Perform other duties relative to coaching as assigned by the principal and the athletic director. Follow all fiscal control act procedures for collected monies. Adhered to athletic and coach handbook as well as state and conference regulations. Requirements: Leadership skills must encourage academic excellence, development of positive community relationships, and communication with a diverse population of students, staff, school and community. Must possess or can obtain a North Carolina Teaching Licensing through North Carolina Department of Public Instruction. Must possess or can obtain a North Carolina CDL license, CPR & AED certifications and complete the NFHS Required Course work within 30 days of hire.

Job.JobTitle: Boy's Assistant Lacrosse Athletic Coach | West Forsyth High School
Job.JobDescription: Assistant Coach: Boys Lacrosse Coach This is a part-time coaching position only. Assistant Lacrosse coaching supplement is based on the athletic special supplement schedule for 3.5 months in accordance with coaching experience. Supplement range for Assistant Lacrosse Coach is $311.00 to $584.00 monthly. Additional coaching duties (if any) with additional supplement pay will be assigned by the Principal and/or Athletic Director. After applying, please also send resume to Mike Pennington, Athletics Director, West Forsyth High School, 1735 Lewisville Clemmons Road, Clemmons, NC 27012, mpennington@wsfcs.k12.nc.us Job Qualifications: Successful high school and/or college lacrosse experience, or other relevant experience. Hold or be eligible to hold a NC commercial drivers license to operate a school activity bus preferred. Will be subject to periodic US Dept. of Transportation random controlled substance screenings. When applying, please include all prior experience.

ORANGE COUNTY SCHOOLS POSITION TITLE Assistant Coach SCHOOL/DEPARTMENT Assigned school/ Assigned sport SALARY Per the appropriate salary schedule FLSA STATUS Exempt REPORTS TO Head Coach, Athletic Director SUPERVISES None WORK WEEK SCHEDULE Monday – Friday; evenings and some weekends WORK HOURS As dictated by the sport and season NUMBER OF MONTHS PER YEAR As dictated by the sport and season POSITION PURPOSE: The Assistant Coach is responsible for providing appropriate coaching guidance to student-athletes related to a particular sport for the duration of the sports season. As part of the Athletic Program, at the assigned school, the Assistant Coach is responsible for teaching and modeling good sportsmanship behaviors, appropriate techniques, and skills related to the particular sport, ensuring safe practices are implemented to protect the students from physical injuries, and for serving as good role models for the students, the school and the school system. KNOWLEDGE OF POSITION: • Thorough knowledge of the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques associated with the athletic program • Thorough knowledge of state and federal requirements regarding physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility for athletic participation • Thorough knowledge of training rules and any other unique regulations about the various sports in which athletes are engaged • Thorough knowledge of guidance and safeguards in place for the safety of athletes to address medical injuries and emergencies • Thorough knowledge of the process for initiating policies and procedures applicable to the athletic program EDUCATION, TRAINING, AND EXPERIENCE • High School Diploma or GED • Additional course work and/or training in a particular sport • Any equivalent combination of training and experience that provides the required knowledge, skills, and abilities PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: • Associate’s Degree or Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited institution in Physical Education or related field • Minimum of three (3) years of coaching experience at the middle or high school level • NC Certification as Teacher, preferably in Physical Education CERTIFICATION AND LICENSE REQUIREMENTS • Must possess a valid driver’s license issued by the NC Department of Motor Vehicles ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Implement, interpret and enforce consistently existing school system, state, and conference regulations 2. Refer all requests of grievances through proper channels 3. Attend all public/staff/departmental meetings for which attendance is required 4. Maintain discipline and work to increase morale and cooperation within the school sports program 5. Assist the Head Coach with scheduling, and providing transportation to tournaments and special sports events 6. Assist with the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times 7. Assist with sporting events and practices with maintenance and school employees 8. Assist with proper safeguard for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites 9. Assist with training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant 10. Attend all practices and games 11. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant while traveling 12. Assist with directing student managers and statisticians on the respective team 13. Assist the Head Coach when determining discipline, delineating procedure concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary, and contacting parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible to participate 14. Accountable to the Head Coach for all equipment 15. Assist with the collection of the cost of any equipment lost or not returned 16. Assist with issuing and storage of equipment and submit an annual inventory and current records to the Head Coach 17. Recommend program area budgetary items for subsequent years to the Head Coach 18. Assist with monitoring and authorizing entrance to equipment rooms and coaches’ offices 19. Assist with examining locker rooms for general cleanliness before and after practices and games 20. Share responsibilities for upkeep and regular care of facilities and fields 21. Assist with ensuring cleanliness and maintenance of specific sports equipment 22. Assist in securing all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving the building if custodians are not on duty 23. Assist with instilling in each player respect for each other, athletic equipment, and school property, including its care and proper use 24. Assist the Head Coach in carrying out their responsibilities 25. Assist with instructing the team members regarding changes in the rules and teaching fundamentals of the sport as outlined by the Head Coach 26. Assist with maintaining a record of team statistics and requirements for lettering (for Head Coach) 27. Work within the basic framework and philosophy of the Head Coach of that sport 28. Attend all staff meetings and carry out scouting assignments as outlined by the Head Coach 29. Arrive early enough before practice, contests, and meetings to assist with preparation adequately and remain long enough afterward to help players with problems and/or be involved in staff discussions 30. Assist with the planning and implementation of both in-season and out-of-season conditioning and weight programs 31. Strive to improve skills by attending clinics and using resources made available by the Head Coach 32. Attend most of the contests, when possible, of other teams in the program 33. Perform other duties as assigned by the Head Coach or Athletic Director DISCLAIMER: The above statements are intended to describe the general purpose and responsibilities assigned to this position. They are not intended to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and skills that may be required of the employees assigned to this position. This description may be revised by the supervisor, with HR review and approval, at any time. WORKING CONDITIONS PHYSICAL DEMANDS Depending on the sport, work may be considered light to heavy requiring the exertion of up to 100 pounds of force. Position may require a lot of standing, walking, kneeling, squatting, pushing, pulling, and running. WORK ENVIRONMENT Employees in this position are required to work in indoor and outdoor environments, with possible exposure to excessive heat and cold, and come into direct contact with OCS staff, students, and the public.

ATHLETIC COACH (to include Cheerleading Under general supervision, assist the board in the fair and just discharge of its obligation to students and staff through performance of the Head Coach role over and beyond the duties of a classroom teacher. Reports to the school principal and athletic director. SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Catawba County Schools Board of Education and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. Knowledge of existing school system, state, and conference regulations; implements them consistently and interprets them for staff. Understand the proper administrative line of command and refer all requests of grievances through proper channels. Awareness of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by staff. Design conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program. Train and inform staff, encourage professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy. Delegate specific duty, supervise implementation, and at season’s end, analyze staff effectiveness and evaluate all assistants. Maintain discipline, adjust grievances, and work to increase morale and cooperation. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for tournament and special sport events. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times. Coordinate program with maintenance and school employees. Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility. Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Advise the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method or procedural changes. Provide training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Give constant attention to a student athlete’s grades and conduct. Presence at all practices and games. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant. Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies. Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries to athletic office within 24 hours. Direct student managers, assistants, and statisticians. Determine discipline, delineate procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contact parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. Assist athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations. Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records. Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing. Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices, authorize who may enter, issue or requisition equipment. Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Be responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty. Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings. Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program. Promote the sport outside the school through news media, community recreation programs, or in any other feasible manner. Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans. Present information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results. Perform other duties as assigned by the school Principal in keeping with Catawba County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes. KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES Must possess administrative and organizational skills in addition to a thorough knowledge of Catawba County Schools athletic policy and procedure as well as the North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes. Ability to communicate effectively with students, staff, parents, and the community. MINIMUM TRAINING AND EXPERIENCE As applicable, must meet requirements established by the North Carolina High School Athletic Association, North Carolina Department of Public Instruction, the Catawba County Board of Education, and school administration. Teacher license and employment as a teacher by the Catawba County Board of Education is strongly preferred. Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. DISCLAIMER The preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications required of employees to this job.

Director with scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for the tournament and special sports events Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times. Coordinate sports events and practices with maintenance and school employees Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites Advise the Athletic Director of any concerns or issues and recommend policy, methods, or procedural changes Provide training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant Give constant attention to a student athlete’s grades and conduct ensuring that the student is eligible academically to participate Attend all practices and games Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies Complete and submit proper paperwork regarding all disabling athletic injuries to the athletic office within 24 hours Direct student managers, assistants, and statisticians Determine discipline, delineate procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary, and contact parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible to participate Assist athletes with their college or advanced educational selection Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment needed Responsible for operating within budget appropriations. Account for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment Submit annual inventory and current records Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices; clarify and authorize who may enter, issue, or requisition equipment Restrict athletes to authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on the general cleanliness of the facility. Ensure cleanliness and maintenance of specific sports equipment Share responsibilities for upkeep and regular care of facilities and fields Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving the building if custodians are not on duty Instill in each player respect for each other, athletic equipment, and school property, including its care and proper use Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings Promote the sport within the school by recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program Promote the sport outside the school through news media, Little League programs, or in any other feasible manner Work with the Athletic Director and Chief Communications Officer to ensure the quality, effectiveness, and validity of any oral or written release to local media Maintain excellent public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans Present information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results Perform other duties as assigned by the Athletic Director or Principal WORKING CONDITIONS PHYSICAL DEMANDS Depending on the sport, work may be considered light to heavy requiring the exertion of up to 100 pounds of force. Position may require a lot of standing, walking, kneeling, squatting, pushing, pulling, and running. WORK ENVIRONMENT Employees in this position are required to work in indoor and outdoor environments, with possible exposure to excessive heat and cold, and come into direct contact with OCS staff, students, and the public. ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS The following signatures acknowledge that the supervisor has verified the accuracy of the position description, has discussed position requirements with the employee, and has advised the employee of work performance expectations Reviewed by: Employee's Signature Date A pproval by: Supervisor's Signature Date The completed and signed position description shall be maintained at the employee’s work location, shall be reviewed with the employee by his or her immediate supervisor, should be revised as required, and shall serve as the basis for all required evaluations. JOB DESCRIPTION GENERAL STATEMENT OF JOB An employee in this position will be a personal example of high moral character and superior teaching standards. The Head JV Baseball Coach will teach good sportsmanship as the number one priority on the athletic field. This Coach will work with the faculty and staff to support player academic and moral success. QUALIFICATIONS NC State Teachers Salary Schedule Local Athletic Supplement (Based on degree and experience) AND/OR Head JV Baseball Coach Plus Local Teacher Supplement $739.20 - $1593.90 per season (Based on exp. as a baseball coach) SALARY INFORMATION Must be licensed or be eligible to receive a NC Teaching License with experience as a baseball coach or extensive knowledge in this area. OR Must have experience as a baseball coach or extensive knowledge in this area to be eligible to serve as a non-faculty coach. The High School Defensive Coordinator is responsible for leading and managing all aspects of the defensive side of the football program. This position plays a vital role in developing student-athletes both on and off the field by fostering discipline, accountability, leadership and teamwork. The Defensive Coordinator works closely with the Head Coach and coaching staff to build a competitive, well prepared, and fundamentally sound defense that reflects the values and standards of Lexington Senior High School. Qualifications - Previous high school or collegiate football coaching experience. Defense preferred. - Strong Knowledge of defensive schemes, coverages, and front alignments. - Ability to teach and communicate effectively with student-athletes. - Demonstrate leadership skills and ability to build positive relationships. - Commitment to developing student-athletes academically, athletically and socially - Ability to work collaboratively with staff, administration, parents, and community stakeholders. Preferred Qualifications - Prior Defensive Coordinator or Head Coaching Experience - Experience using film breakdown and scouting software (Hudl) - Teaching license or eligibility for employment with the school district.

The High School Offensive Coordinator is responsible for leading and managing all aspects of the offensive side of the football program. This position plays a vital role in developing student-athletes both on and off the field by fostering discipline, accountability, leadership and teamwork. The Offensive Coordinator works closely with the Head Coach and coaching staff to build a competitive, well-prepared, and fundamentally sound offense that reflects the values and standards of Lexington Senior High School. Qualifications -Previous high school or collegiate football coaching experience (offense preferred) - Strong Knowledge of offensive schemes, formations and situational football. - Ability to teach and communicate effectively with student-athletes. - Demonstrate Leadership skills and ability to build positive relationships. - Commitment to developing student-athletes academically, athletically and socially. - Ability to work collaboratively with staff, administration, parents and community stakeholders. Preferred Qualifications -Prior Offensive Coordinator or Head Coaching Experience - Experience using film breakdown and scouting software (Hudl) - Teaching License or eligibilty for employment within the school district.

The High School Assistant Football Coach supports the Head Coach in all aspects of the football program, contributing to the development of the student-athletes on and off the field. The position plays a critical role in skill instruction, practice organizaton, game preparation, and fostering a culture of discipline, sportsmanship and accoutabilty. The Assistant Coach is expected to serve as a positive role model and uphold the values and standards of Lexington Senior High School. Qualifications - Previous playing or coaching experience at the High School or Collegiate level. - Strong Knowledge of Football Fundamentals, rules and strategies - Ability to motivate and mentor high school students Preffered to have a teaching license or eligibilty of employment with the school district.

The Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) role at Lincoln Public Sch. is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Lincoln Public Sch., teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Lincoln Public Sch., the Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Coach, Assistant Track Coach (2 positions) at Lincoln Public Sch. is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

North Star High School Varsity Head Dance Coach Requires Teacher and/or coach who holds a Nebraska Department of Education Teacher or Administrative Certificate or a Nebraska Department of Education Special Services Coaching Permit. Lincoln North Star High School is seeking a varsity head coach to provide qualified leadership for the Dance Team. Good communication and organizational skills are required as well as the ability to develop positive, productive relationships with student-athletes, parents, and the community. Must be able to provide quality instruction of fundamental skills as well as have a comprehensive knowledge of the sport of Dance. The successful candidate must have the ability to lead and enhance the ongoing development, improvement, and participation in the program. Be responsible for inspecting and maintaining the inventory and equipment; reporting contest results; and must know, implement, and enforce national, state, and local athletic policies. This position is subject to a veterans preference. Job Description: Coach

Lincoln Northeast Rockets: Reserve Girls Tennis The applicant must show strong leadership skills, works well with students and adults, and values hard work and is committed to the activity and has knowledge of the sport of tennis. The applicant must work well with others and is willing to listen and adapt to plans from the Varsity level. This position will focus on teaching basics and cultivating a growth mindset for players who are fairly new to the sport. This position will require acting as the head coach at Reserve level matches, and to help train players to progress through the levels of high school tennis. Requires certified teacher or coach who holds a Nebraska Department of Education Teacher or Administrative Certificate or a Nebraska Department of Education Special Services This position also require NFHS Certifications in Concusion Awareness, Sudden Cardiac Arrest, and Heat Illness. Also this position requires CPR Certification. These certfications may be completed after the hiring is confirmed. This position is subject to a veterans preference. Job Description: Coach

Job.JobDescription: BENNINGTON PUBLIC SCHOOLS Position Description Position Title: Assistant High School Track and Field Coach (2026-2027) JOB DESCRIPTION JOB TITLE: Assistant High School Track and Field Coach - Bennington High School REPORTS TO: Activities Director TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: Part-Time I. ACCOUNTABILITY OBJECTIVES: To instruct athletes in fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. The student shall also receive instruction that will end to the formation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, self-confidence, resilience, and cooperation with teammates. Regular, dependable, in-person attendance is an essential function of the job. II. POSITION CHARACTERISTICS: Recruited by: Athletic Director/Principal Wages: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Paid Vacation: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Paid Holidays: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Sick Leave: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Work Day: Hours to be determined by Activities Director/Principal Pay Day: As determined by Negotiated Agreement. III. POSITION RELATIONSHIPS: Reports to Activities Director Coordinates with: Activities Director, Building Principal, Custodians, and Transportation Supervisor. IV. POSITION RESPONSIBILITIES: Be responsible for the development and formulation of his/her program in grades 9-12 Oversee a systematic skill development program for all athletes in the program Recommend assistant coaches and coach’s aides and officials to the Activities Director Essential Functions The essential functions of the position include: regular, dependable attendance on the job; the ability to perform the identified tasks and to possess and utilize the identified knowledge, skills and abilities to perform the identified work activities; and, the ability to perform the identified physical requirements as outlined in working environment. Working Environment The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is required to lift and pull or push articles up to 50 pounds. He/she sits most of the time but must walk frequently and, at times, stand. The employee is required to reach with his/her hands and continuously to repeat the same hand, arm, or finger motion many times as in typing. Manual and finger dexterity are essential to the job. He/she is constantly working to meet deadlines with severe time restraints. He/she must see and read fine print such as typed or handwritten materials and have good depth perception (fine muscle control). The employee must, also, be able to hear conversation in a noisy environment and to communicate clearly and concisely through speech. __ The information contained in this job description is for compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and is not an exhaustive list of the duties performed for this position. Additional duties are performed by the individuals currently holding this position and additional duties may be assigned. EOE

Scarborough Public Sch. is looking for a Softball coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job Title : 7th Grade Softball Coach 2025-2026 Date Available : Immediately Requirements: Previous experience playing and/or coaching softball at or above the high school level. First Aid & CPR certification. Maine Department of Education valid fingerprinting and CHRC approval. NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching Principles Certification NFHS Heat Illness Prevention Certification NFHS Concussion in Sport Certification NFHS Sudden Cardiac Arrest Certification NFHS COVID-19 Certification NFHS Protecting Students from Abuse Other certification and trainings may be deemed necessary by the Department, Board or Maine Principals Association Job Description : To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the student shall receive instruction that will lead to the formulation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline and self-confidence. Ensure and provide the safest possible competitive & educational environment for all student athletes involved. Assist in the overall development of the Scarborough Softball program as assigned by the Department of Athletics. Duties : Abide by the Scarborough Middle School Athletic Departments Coaches Handbook Abide by all Maine Principals Association rules and regulations Follow and implement all rules and policies within the school & athletic handbooks Equipment inventory & management May need to communicate travel arrangement & schedule with Athletic Department staff Implement school athletic Code of Conduct Make sure roster and students comply with Athletic Department Ensure athletic participation fee payment by athletes in conjunction with AD's office Help in the distribution of team rosters, contest schedules and descriptions of contest locations to all players and parents Properly communicate with students and parents as it relates to the SMS communication guide Monitor athlete attendance/behavior/academic status as communicated by the athletic department Complete all paperwork assigned by the department. Communicate all student-athlete injuries to the Athletic Trainer and/or athletic office Attend all school Athletic Department meetings as assigned by the Athletic Director All others as assigned by the Director of Athletics and the Athletic Department Maine State Law requires ALL school employees to be fingerprinted and pass a Criminal History Record Check (CHRC). A copy of your CHRC MUST be uploaded as part of your application to be considered. For more information about Maine's Criminal History Record Check (CHRC) requirements, please see the Maine Department of Education website at http://maine.gov/doe/cert/fingerprinting/index.html Application Instructions: Scarborough Public Schools does not accept paper applications or supporting materials. All applications and supporting materials are to be completed via Frontline Application Tracking at www.applitrack.com/scarborough/onlineapp , or www.scarboroughschools.org under Employment Opportunities. Application Deadline: Until suitable candidate is found. Students learn in a positive atmosphere where dedicated educators strive to meet individual student needs throughout the school year and beyond. For more information on Scarborough Public Schools, please see our website at w ww.scarboroughschools.org

The Assistant Coach - Varsity Football position at Bangor High School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Assistant Coach - Varsity Football prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Bangor High School, the Assistant Coach - Varsity Football works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Assistant Coach - Varsity Football encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Assistant Coach - Varsity Football at Bangor High School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

The Freshman Girls Volleyball Coach at Rockford High School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the Freshman Girls Volleyball Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Rockford High School, the Freshman Girls Volleyball Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The Freshman Girls Volleyball Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Rockford High School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The Freshman Girls Volleyball Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: 1. High school graduate or equivalent. 2. Applicants must have experience in high school level coaching or playing at college or university level in sport to which they are applying. 3. Applicant must have a time schedule compatible with that of the high school. 4. CPR and/or first aide training preferred. 5. Demonstrated ability to work with parents and children.
REPORTS TO: Assistant Principal of Activities and Athletics RATE OF PAY: According to the Master Agreement - Schedule "B" BEGINNING DATE: Fall 2026
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. The assistant coach will be responsible for the entire organization of the sport to which he/she is assigned. 2. The assistant coach will be directly responsible for all equipment in his/her sport. A complete inventory of all equipment will be filed with the director at the end of each season. 3. The assistant coach will be responsible for all awards presented in his/her sport and a season summary. 4. Bending, lifting, and overhead work required. 5. Performs other tasks as assigned. This description is intended to describe the essential job functions, the general supplemental functions and the essential requirements for the performance of this job. It is not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities and requirements of a person so classified. Other functions may be assigned and management retains the right to add or change the duties at any time.
EVALUATION: Performance of this position will be evaluated annually.
METHOD OF APPLICATION: Internal and external applicants should complete the online application in the Frontline system. External applicants will be hired and paid by EDUStaff. www.EDUStaff.org
DEADLINE FOR APPLICATION: Open until filled POSTED: 03/09/26
Job.SalaryRange: According to the Master Agreement - Schedule "B"

Job.JobDescription: Purpose Statement: Extra-curricular activities are a vital extension of the student experience. The Assistant Football Coach works under the direction of the Head Coach to inspire, motivate, and teach students football-specific skills while fostering positive character traits, teamwork, and relationships. Key Responsibilities: Support the Head Coach in managing all aspects of the football program. Assist in developing and implementing effective practice schedules, strategies, and game plans. Promote and uphold sportsmanship and team values. Foster positive relationships with students, staff, and the community. Ensure student eligibility and adherence to district and state policies. Collaborate with the Head Coach, athletic directors, and other staff as needed. Education and Certification: Assistant Coaches must have completed 60 or more college credit hours prior to coaching. Evening and weekend commitments are expected during the season. We look forward to receiving your application and welcoming a passionate coach to our team!

Extra-curricular activities are a vital part of enriching the student experience. As an Assistant Coach, you'll inspire, motivate, and teach students specific skills related to their chosen activity while fostering positive character development and meaningful relationships.
Qualifications We're looking for passionate and dedicated individuals who meet the following criteria:
Eligible Applicants: FHSD Certified Employees FHSD Non-Certified Employees Qualified External Candidates Education Education Requirement: Completion of 60 or more college credit hours prior to coaching.
This is an excellent opportunity to make a meaningful impact on students' lives beyond the classroom. We look forward to your application and the possibility of welcoming you to our team!
Job.Experience: 60 or more college credit hours

Job.JobTitle: High School Cheer Assistant Coach for 2026-2027
Job.JobDescription:
The High School Cheer Assistant Coach for 2026 role at Raytown South High School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the High School Cheer Assistant Coach for 2026 is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Raytown South High School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The High School Cheer Assistant Coach for 2026 listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the High School Cheer Assistant Coach for 2026 encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The High School Cheer Assistant Coach for 2026 follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Raytown South High School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the High School Cheer Assistant Coach for 2026 at Raytown South High School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.
Job.Experience: Minimum 3 years coaching experience, 3-5 years head coaching experience preferred.

Job.JobTitle: High School Flag Football Assistant Girls Coach
Job.JobDescription:
The High School Flag Football Assistant Girls Coach at Raytown South High School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the High School Flag Football Assistant Girls Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Raytown South High School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The High School Flag Football Assistant Girls Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The High School Flag Football Assistant Girls Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Raytown South High School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Raytown South High School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the High School Flag Football Assistant Girls Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the High School Flag Football Assistant Girls Coach role at Raytown South High School.
Job.Experience: Minimum 3 years coaching experience, 3-5 years head coaching experience preferred.

Job.JobTitle: High School Soccer Assistant Coach Boys for 2026-2027
Job.JobDescription:
The High School Soccer Assistant Coach Boys for 2026 at Raytown South High School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the High School Soccer Assistant Coach Boys for 2026 plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Raytown South High School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The High School Soccer Assistant Coach Boys for 2026 expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the High School Soccer Assistant Coach Boys for 2026 supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the High School Soccer Assistant Coach Boys for 2026 may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Raytown South High School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.
Job.JobType: Athletics/Activities/Activity: HS, Soccer Assistant Coach Boys

Job.JobTitle: High School Softball Assistant Coach for 2026-2027
Job.JobDescription:
The High School Softball Assistant Coach for 2026 at Raytown South High School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the High School Softball Assistant Coach for 2026 is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Raytown South High School, the High School Softball Assistant Coach for 2026 helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The High School Softball Assistant Coach for 2026 may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Raytown South High School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The High School Softball Assistant Coach for 2026 follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.
Job.Experience: Minimum 3 years coaching experience, 3-5 years head coaching experience preferred.

Job.JobDescription:
The High School Assistant Track Coach position at Raytown South High School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the High School Assistant Track Coach prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Raytown South High School, the High School Assistant Track Coach works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The High School Assistant Track Coach encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the High School Assistant Track Coach at Raytown South High School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.
Job.JobType: Athletics/Activities/Activity: HS, Track Assistant Coach G/B
Job.Experience: Minimum 3 years coaching experience, 3-5 years head coaching experience preferred.

Job.JobTitle: Golf Athletic Coach - Mineral Springs Middle School
Job.JobDescription:
The Golf Athletic Coach - Mineral Springs Middle School position at Mineral Springs Middle School is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Golf Athletic Coach - Mineral Springs Middle School is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Mineral Springs Middle School, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Golf Athletic Coach - Mineral Springs Middle School encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Golf Athletic Coach - Mineral Springs Middle School also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Golf Athletic Coach - Mineral Springs Middle School role at Mineral Springs Middle School is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

Job Description
JOHNSTON COUNTY PUBLIC SCHOOLS
JOB DESCRIPTION
POSITION: Non-Faculty Coach
LOCATION: Administrative and Auxiliary Services
SALARY: JCPS Coach Supplement Scale
REPORTS TO: Principal and School-Based Athletic Director
JOB GOALS: An employee in this position will exhibit high moral character and execute scholastic based athletic principals. The Non-Faculty Coach will ensure safety of athletes while participating; teach good sportsmanship; establish a welcoming environment for all; and positively influence the students they work with. This Coach will work with administration, faculty, and staff to work towards the district’s goals regarding the whole child, academics, and athletics.
QUALIFICATIONS AND ATTRIBUTES:
Graduation from high school or equivalent required. Previous successful coaching experience preferred. Knowledgeable of all athletic policies approved by the Johnston County Schools’ Board of Education. Knowledgeable of existing system, state, and conference regulations. Ability and willingness to organize and supervise athletic team members. Must possess superior knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. Willingness to obtain a CDL to transport students on an approved activity bus to contests. Also, maintain certifications in order to do so, throughout tenure. Knowledgeable of the proper administrative line of command and the referral process for requests or grievances utilizing the proper chain of command. Knowledgeable of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance.
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:
Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility. Maintain or acquire all necessary coaching prerequisites annually or as they expire listed by NCDPI, NCHSAA, & Johnston County Public Schools. Promote the sport within the school through holding interest meetings and encouraging multisport athletic opportunities. Maintain all medical emergency information needed for each athlete, while also understanding emergency protocols if they were to arise. Promote the sport outside the school by being involved in the community, getting involved in coaching organizations, and attending required meetings. Provide quality and approved communication to the public. Maintain good public relations with members of the community, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans. Through the Athletic Director, prepare and release information to the public including schedules, tournaments, and results. Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught in assigned duty. Provides training, rules, and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Assist with monitoring student’s athlete’s grades and conduct. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each athletic participant through regular attendance at games and practices. Follow policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies. Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries within 24 hours. Assist athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. Collaborate with the Athletic Director to inform and determine discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary, and contacts parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. Assist the Head Coach in facilitating required pre-season meeting. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for sports events. Collaborate with the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method, or procedural changes. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates athletic program with maintenance and school employees. Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arranges for issuing, storing, and reconditioning or equipment and submits annual inventory and current records. Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing. Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices. Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on the general cleanliness of the facility. Is responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sports equipment. Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving the building. Instill in each player respect for equipment and school property, its care, and proper use. Job Description
JOHNSTON COUNTY PUBLIC SCHOOLS
JOB DESCRIPTION
POSITION: Non-Faculty Coach
LOCATION: Administrative and Auxiliary Services
SALARY: JCPS Coach Supplement Scale
REPORTS TO: Principal and School-Based Athletic Director
JOB GOALS: An employee in this position will exhibit high moral character and execute scholastic based athletic principals. The Non-Faculty Coach will ensure safety of athletes while participating; teach good sportsmanship; establish a welcoming environment for all; and positively influence the students they work with. This Coach will work with administration, faculty, and staff to work towards the district’s goals regarding the whole child, academics, and athletics.
QUALIFICATIONS AND ATTRIBUTES:
Graduation from high school or equivalent required. Previous successful coaching experience preferred. Knowledgeable of all athletic policies approved by the Johnston County Schools’ Board of Education. Knowledgeable of existing system, state, and conference regulations. Ability and willingness to organize and supervise athletic team members. Must possess superior knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. Willingness to obtain a CDL to transport students on an approved activity bus to contests. Also, maintain certifications in order to do so, throughout tenure. Knowledgeable of the proper administrative line of command and the referral process for requests or grievances utilizing the proper chain of command. Knowledgeable of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance.
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:
Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility. Maintain or acquire all necessary coaching prerequisites annually or as they expire listed by NCDPI, NCHSAA, & Johnston County Public Schools. Promote the sport within the school through holding interest meetings and encouraging multisport athletic opportunities. Maintain all medical emergency information needed for each athlete, while also understanding emergency protocols if they were to arise. Promote the sport outside the school by being involved in the community, getting involved in coaching organizations, and attending required meetings. Provide quality and approved communication to the public. Maintain good public relations with members of the community, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans. Through the Athletic Director, prepare and release information to the public including schedules, tournaments, and results. Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught in assigned duty. Provides training, rules, and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Assist with monitoring student’s athlete’s grades and conduct. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each athletic participant through regular attendance at games and practices. Follow policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies. Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries within 24 hours. Assist athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. Collaborate with the Athletic Director to inform and determine discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary, and contacts parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. Assist the Head Coach in facilitating required pre-season meeting. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for sports events. Collaborate with the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method, or procedural changes. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates athletic program with maintenance and school employees. Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arranges for issuing, storing, and reconditioning or equipment and submits annual inventory and current records. Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing. Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices. Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on the general cleanliness of the facility. Is responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sports equipment. Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving the building. Instill in each player respect for equipment and school property, its care, and proper use. Perform such other duties as assigned by the Principal and or Superintendent of schools in keeping with Johnston County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes.
