Job Description
REPORTS TO: Principal
SUPERVISES: May coordinate and direct the activities of teacher assistants.
PURPOSE: To plan, organize and present instruction and instructional environments that help students learn subject matter and skills that will contribute to their educational and social development.
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
A. MAJOR FUNCTION: Management of Instructional Time
The teacher has materials, supplies, and equipment for each lesson ready at the start of the lesson or instructional activity; gets the class started quickly; gets students on task quickly at the beginning of each lesson; maintains a high level of student time-on-task.
B. MAJOR FUNCTION: Management of Student Behavior
The teacher has established a set of rules and procedures that govern the handling of routine administrative matters; has established a set of rules and procedures that govern student verbal participation and talk during different types of activities—whole-class instruction, small group instruction, etc.; has established a set of rules and procedures that govern student movement in the classroom during different types of instructional and non- instructional activities; frequently monitors the behavior of all students during whole-class, small group, and seat work activities and during transitions between instructional activities; stops inappropriate behavior promptly and consistently, yet maintains the dignity of the student.
C. MAJOR FUNCTION: Instructional Presentation
The teacher begins lesson or instructional activity with a review of previous materials; introduces the lesson or instructional activity and specific learning objectives when appropriate; speaks fluently and precisely; presents the lesson or instructional activity using concepts and language understandable to the students; provides relevant examples and demonstrations to illustrate concepts and skills; assigns tasks that students handle with a high rate of success; asks appropriate levels of questions that students handle with a high rate of success; conducts lesson or instructional activity at a brisk pace, slowing presentations when necessary for student understanding but avoiding slowdowns; makes transitions between lessons and between instructional activities within lessons efficiently and smoothly; makes sure that the assignment is clear; summarizes the main point(s) of the lesson at the end of the lesson or instructional activity.
GCS, Rev. 2/06
D. MAJOR FUNCTION: Instructional Monitoring of Student Performance
The teacher maintains clear, firm and reasonable work standards and due dates; circulates during class work to check all students’ performance; routinely uses oral, written, and other work products to check student progress; poses questions clearly and one at a time.
E. MAJOR FUNCTION: Instructional Feedback
The teacher provides feedback on the correctness or incorrectness of in-class work to encourage student growth; regularly provides prompt feedback on assigned out-of-class work; affirms a correct oral response appropriately, and moves on; provides sustaining feedback after an incorrect response or no response by probing, repeating the question, giving a clue, or allowing more time.
F. MAJOR FUNCTION: Facilitating Instruction
The teacher has an instructional plan which is compatible with the school and system- wide curricular goals; uses diagnostic information obtained from tests and other assessment procedures to develop and revise objectives and/or tasks; maintains accurate records to document student performance; has instructional plan that matches/aligns objectives, learning strategies, assessment and student needs at the appropriate level of difficulty; uses available human and material resources to support the instructional program.
G. MAJOR FUNCTION: Interacting Within the Educational Environment
The teacher treats all students in a fair and equitable manner; interacts effectively with students, co-workers, parents, and community.
H. MAJOR FUNCTION: Performing Non-Instructional Duties
The teacher carries out non-instructional duties as assigned and/or as need is perceived; adheres to established laws, policies, rules, and regulations; follows a plan for professional development and demonstrates evidence of growth.
ADDITIONAL JOB FUNCTIONS
Performs other related work as required.
MINIMUM TRAINING AND EXPERIENCE
Degree in education or in a related area that will qualify for licensure as a teacher by the North Carolina Department of Public Instruction.
MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS OR STANDARDS REQUIRED TO PERFORM ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS
Physical Requirements: Must be able to use a variety of equipment and classroom tools such as computers, copiers, typewriters, calculators, pencils, scissors, and equipment for children with special needs, etc. Must be able to exert a negligible amount of force frequently or constantly to lift, carr

Orange Middle School Athletics is looking for a Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION DESCRIPTION POSITION TITLE Assistant Athletic Director SCHOOL/DEPARTMENT Assigned School SALARY Per the appropriate salary schedule FLSA STATUS Exempt REPORTS TO School Athletic Director SUPERVISES Coaches WORK WEEK SCHEDULE Monday - Friday; some evenings and weekends WORK HOURS 40 Minimum NUMBER OF MONTHS PER YEAR 10
POSITION PURPOSE: The Assistant Athletic Director assists the School Athletic Director regarding the provision and supervision of athletic programs at the assigned school.
MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES • Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Orange County Schools Board of Education • Thorough knowledge of existing system, state, and conference regulations • Thorough understanding of the proper administrative chain of command • Thorough knowledge of the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques associated with the athletic program • Thorough knowledge of state and federal requirements regarding physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility for athletic participation • Thorough knowledge of training rules and any other unique regulations of the various sports in which athletes are engaged • Thorough knowledge of guidance and safeguards in place for the safety of athletes to address medical injuries and emergencies • Thorough knowledge of the process for initiating policies and procedures applicable to the athletic program EDUCATION, TRAINING, AND EXPERIENCE • Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited institution in Physical Education or a related field • Minimum of three (3) years of classroom experience as a Physical Education teacher at the middle or high school level • Any equivalent combination of training and experience that provides the required knowledge, skills, and abilities CERTIFICATION AND LICENSE REQUIREMENTS • NC Teacher Certification in Physical Education • Must possess a valid driver’s license issued by the NC Department of Motor Vehicles PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: • Master’s Degree or additional credits beyond a Bachelor’s Degree from an accredited institution in a related field ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Responsible for the implementation by the entire staff of the sports program, according to the OCS Athletic Policy and Board of Education policies 2. Implement existing system, state, and conference athletic regulations with fidelity 3. Utilize the proper administrative line of command and refer all requests and grievances through proper channels 4. Attend all required public/staff/departmental meetings 5. Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by staff 6. Design conferences, clinics, and staff meetings to ensure staff awareness of the overall program as needed 7. Encourage staff professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy 8. Delegate specific duties, supervise implementation, and at season’s end; analyze staff effectiveness and help evaluate staff as needed 9. Maintain discipline, address grievances, and work to increase morale and cooperation 10. Assist the Athletic Director with scheduling, providing transportation, and communicating requirements for tournaments and special sporting events 11. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices, and adhere to scheduled facility times 12. Coordinate programs with maintenance and school employees 13. Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility 14. Provide proper safeguards for the maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites 15. Advise the Athletic Director and Athletic Supervisor regarding any concerns and recommend policy, method, or procedural changes 16. Provide training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant 17. Monitor and give constant attention to student-athletes’ grades and conduct 18. Must be present at all practices and games 19. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant 20. Share responsibilities for upkeep and regular care of all facilities and fields 21. Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies 22. Complete and submit proper paperwork regarding all disabling athletic injuries to the athletic office within 24 hours of the occurrence 23. Determine discipline by school system policies 24. Communicate procedures regarding due process entitlements when the enforcement of discipline is necessary 25. Contact parents when a student is dropped from a team or becomes ineligible to participate 26. Assist athletes with their college or advanced educational selections 27. Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season 28. Recommend guidelines regarding type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment 29. Assist with operating within budget appropriations. 30. Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing and/or storing 31. Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices; authorize who may enter 32. Issue and/or requisition equipment 33. Limit student-athlete presence to authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times 34. Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games 35. Check on the general cleanliness of the facility, and be responsible for the cleanliness and maintenance of specific sports equipment 36. Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving the building if custodians are not on duty 37. Instill in each player a respect for each other, equipment, and school property, emphasizing care and proper use of equipment 38. Help organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for the pre-season meeting 39. Promote the sport within the school by recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program 40. Promote the sport outside the school via news media, Little League programs, or in any other feasible manner 41. Maintain good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and spectators 42. Present information to the news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results, if needed 43. Perform other duties as assigned by the Athletic Director, Athletic Supervisor, and/or School Principal
WORKING CONDITIONS PHYSICAL DEMANDS Substantial standing, walking, moving, climbing, carrying, bending, kneeling, crawling, reaching, handling, pushing, and pulling. Work is considered moderate physical work requiring the exertion of up to 50 pounds of force. WORK ENVIRONMENT Employees in this position are required to work in indoor and outdoor environments and come into direct contact with OCS staff, students, and the public.
ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS
Job.Experience: Minimum of three (3) years of classroom experience as a Physical Education teacher at the middle or high school level

The Assistant Flag Football Coach role at Jordan High School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Assistant Flag Football Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Jordan High School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Assistant Flag Football Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Assistant Flag Football Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Assistant Flag Football Coach follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Jordan High School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Assistant Flag Football Coach at Jordan High School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

The Head Girls Track Coach role at Asheville High School is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Head Girls Track Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Asheville High School, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Head Girls Track Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Asheville High School, the Head Girls Track Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Head Girls Track Coach at Asheville High School is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

POSITION TITLE: Head Coach - Baseball LOCATION: Topsail Middle School PAY GRADE: District Coaching Supplement Scale FLSA STATUS: Non-Exempt REPORTS TO: Athletic Director/Principal WORK WEEK SCHEDULE: Monday - Friday WORK HOURS: 3:00 PM- 8:00 PM; varies depending on season, includes nights and weekends MONTHS/YR: Varies - Part-Time POSITION PURPOSE To instruct student athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the student shall receive instruction that will lead to the formulation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline and self-confidence. MINIMUM QUALIFICATION STANDARDS KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES • Ability to organize and supervise a total sports program • Previous successful coaching experience • Substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and at the same time must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the sport • Must know and follow the rules of the North Carolina High School Athletic Association, the North Carolina Department of Public Instruction, and the Pender County Board of Education EDUCATION, TRAINING, AND EXPERIENCE • Successful completion of the NFHS “Fundamentals of Coaching” certification course • Annual completion of the NFHS “Concussions in Sports” course or an equivalent concussion curriculum prior to the first date of practice in their sport • If coaching cheerleading, must complete the NFHS “American Association of Cheerleading Coaches Spirit Safety Certification” course prior to the first date of practice • Current CPR/AED certification; recommended to successfully complete the NFHS “First Aid for Coaches” course • Must complete the NFHS “Sudden Cardiac Arrest” Course after June 1 and prior to the first practice • All high school head coaches must attend one of the NCHSAA state rules clinics in their assigned sport. CERTIFICATION AND LICENSE REQUIREMENTS • Must hold and maintain a valid motor vehicle operator’s license according to the State of NC requirements SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS • Ability to work flexible hours (evenings, weekends) PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS • Hold valid driver license for driving an activity bus in Pender County Schools • Be a bona fide member of the school faculty • Be an Accredited Interscholastic Coach or Certified Interscholastic Coach by the NFHS • Playing experience at the high school and college level ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES • Holds organizational meetings for team prospects and encourages potential athletes to participate in the sport • Assesses a player’s skill and assigns team positions • Develops a regular practice schedule and organizes practice time to provide both team and individual development • Works with the athletic director to schedule facilities for practices and competitions • Assigns duties to an assistant coach as necessary • Coaches and instructs players, individually or in groups, regarding the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport • Observes players, during competition and practice to determine the needs for individual or team improvement • Determines game strategy based on the team’s capabilities • Establishes and maintains standards of student behavior and provides proper supervision of athletes at all times • Monitors the academic performance of team members to ensure that eligibility standards are met; and encourages student athletes to maintain high academic standards • Follows established procedures in the event of an athlete’s injury • Conferences with parents/guardians, as necessary, regarding the athletic performance of their student • Follows state, conference, and district regulations governing the athletic program • Models and maintains appropriate conduct and behavior towards players, officials, and spectators • Acts a team representative and promotes the sport by communicating with booster clubs, service organizations, and the school community • Maintains and cares for equipment, supplies, and uniforms, including correct inventory of same • Assist with the maintenance of athletic playing areas (i.e. mowing grass, picking up debris, operating a weed eater) • Attends staff development, clinics, and other professional activities to improve performance • Performs other duties as assigned by the Principal and Athletic Director WORKING CONDITIONS PHYSICAL DEMANDS • Must be able to use a variety of office equipment such as computers and copiers • Must be able to frequently lift, carry, push, pull or otherwise move objects up to 20 pounds Due to the amount of time spent working on the computer, standing and walking, physical requirements are consistent with those for light work WORK ENVIRONMENT • Must be able to work in office and public school environments, and come into direct contact with staff and students • Travel is required to schools and Central Services offices DISCLAIMER: The above statements are intended to describe the general purpose and responsibilities assigned to this position. They are not intended to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and skills that may be required of the employees assigned to this position. This description may be revised by the supervisor, with HR review and approval, at any time.

POSITION TITLE: Head Coach - Football LOCATION: Surf City Middle School PAY GRADE: District Coaching Supplement Scale FLSA STATUS: Non-Exempt REPORTS TO: Athletic Director/Principal WORK WEEK SCHEDULE: Monday - Friday WORK HOURS: 3:00 PM- 8:00 PM; varies depending on season, includes nights and weekends MONTHS/YR: Varies - Part-Time POSITION PURPOSE To instruct student athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the student shall receive instruction that will lead to the formulation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline and self-confidence. MINIMUM QUALIFICATION STANDARDS KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES • Ability to organize and supervise a total sports program • Previous successful coaching experience • Substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and at the same time must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the sport • Must know and follow the rules of the North Carolina High School Athletic Association, the North Carolina Department of Public Instruction, and the Pender County Board of Education EDUCATION, TRAINING, AND EXPERIENCE • Successful completion of the NFHS “Fundamentals of Coaching” certification course • Annual completion of the NFHS “Concussions in Sports” course or an equivalent concussion curriculum prior to the first date of practice in their sport • If coaching cheerleading, must complete the NFHS “American Association of Cheerleading Coaches Spirit Safety Certification” course prior to the first date of practice • Current CPR/AED certification; recommended to successfully complete the NFHS “First Aid for Coaches” course • Must complete the NFHS “Sudden Cardiac Arrest” Course after June 1 and prior to the first practice • All high school head coaches must attend one of the NCHSAA state rules clinics in their assigned sport. CERTIFICATION AND LICENSE REQUIREMENTS • Must hold and maintain a valid motor vehicle operator’s license according to the State of NC requirements SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS • Ability to work flexible hours (evenings, weekends) PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS • Hold valid driver license for driving an activity bus in Pender County Schools • Be a bona fide member of the school faculty • Be an Accredited Interscholastic Coach or Certified Interscholastic Coach by the NFHS • Playing experience at the high school and college level ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES • Holds organizational meetings for team prospects and encourages potential athletes to participate in the sport • Assesses a player’s skill and assigns team positions • Develops a regular practice schedule and organizes practice time to provide both team and individual development • Works with the athletic director to schedule facilities for practices and competitions • Assigns duties to an assistant coach as necessary • Coaches and instructs players, individually or in groups, regarding the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport • Observes players, during competition and practice to determine the needs for individual or team improvement • Determines game strategy based on the team’s capabilities • Establishes and maintains standards of student behavior and provides proper supervision of athletes at all times • Monitors the academic performance of team members to ensure that eligibility standards are met; and encourages student athletes to maintain high academic standards • Follows established procedures in the event of an athlete’s injury • Conferences with parents/guardians, as necessary, regarding the athletic performance of their student • Follows state, conference, and district regulations governing the athletic program • Models and maintains appropriate conduct and behavior towards players, officials, and spectators • Acts a team representative and promotes the sport by communicating with booster clubs, service organizations, and the school community • Maintains and cares for equipment, supplies, and uniforms, including correct inventory of same • Assist with the maintenance of athletic playing areas (i.e. mowing grass, picking up debris, operating a weed eater) • Attends staff development, clinics, and other professional activities to improve performance • Performs other duties as assigned by the Principal and Athletic Director WORKING CONDITIONS PHYSICAL DEMANDS • Must be able to use a variety of office equipment such as computers and copiers • Must be able to frequently lift, carry, push, pull or otherwise move objects up to 20 pounds Due to the amount of time spent working on the computer, standing and walking, physical requirements are consistent with those for light work WORK ENVIRONMENT • Must be able to work in office and public school environments, and come into direct contact with staff and students • Travel is required to schools and Central Services offices DISCLAIMER: The above statements are intended to describe the general purpose and responsibilities assigned to this position. They are not intended to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and skills that may be required of the employees assigned to this position. This description may be revised by the supervisor, with HR review and approval, at any time.

The Head Golf Coach position at Hillside High School is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Head Golf Coach is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Hillside High School, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Head Golf Coach encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Head Golf Coach also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Head Golf Coach role at Hillside High School is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

The Head Girls Soccer Coach at Lakewood Montessori Middle School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the Head Girls Soccer Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Lakewood Montessori Middle School, the Head Girls Soccer Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The Head Girls Soccer Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Lakewood Montessori Middle School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The Head Girls Soccer Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

The Head Softball Coach at Githens Middle School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Head Softball Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Githens Middle School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Head Softball Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Head Softball Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Githens Middle School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Githens Middle School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Head Softball Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Head Softball Coach role at Githens Middle School.

The Assistant Cheerleading Coach role at Lowe's Grove Middle School emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Assistant Cheerleading Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Lowe's Grove Middle School, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Assistant Cheerleading Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Lowe's Grove Middle School at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Assistant Cheerleading Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Assistant Cheerleading Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Assistant Cheerleading Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Lowe's Grove Middle School.

The Assistant Softball Coach at Githens Middle School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Assistant Softball Coach manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Githens Middle School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Assistant Softball Coach demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Assistant Softball Coach follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Assistant Softball Coach at Githens Middle School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

The Assistant Girls Soccer Coach role at Lakewood Montessori Middle School is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Assistant Girls Soccer Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Lakewood Montessori Middle School, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Assistant Girls Soccer Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Lakewood Montessori Middle School, the Assistant Girls Soccer Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Assistant Girls Soccer Coach at Lakewood Montessori Middle School is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

Job.JobTitle: Volunteer Assistant Volleyball Coach / ATHLETIC COACH
Job.JobDescription:
ATHLETIC COACH (to include Cheerleading)
Under general supervision, assist the board in the fair and just discharge of its obligation to students and staff through performance of the Head Coach role over and beyond the duties of a classroom teacher. Reports to the school principal and athletic director.
SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Through knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Catawba County Schools Board of Education and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the sports program.
Knowledge of existing school system, state, and conference regulations; implements them consistently and interprets them for staff.
Understand the proper administrative line of command and refer all requests of grievances through proper channels. Awareness of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance.
Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by staff. Design conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program.
Train and inform staff, encourage professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy.
Delegate specific duty, supervise implementation, and at season's end, analyze staff effectiveness and evaluate all assistants.
Maintain discipline, adjust grievances, and work to increase morale and cooperation.
Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for tournament and special sport events.
Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times. Coordinate program with maintenance and school employees.
Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility.
Provide proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Advise the Athletic Director and recommend policy, method or procedural changes.
Provide training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant.
Give constant attention to a student athlete's grades and conduct.
Presence at all practices and games. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant.
Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies.
Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries to athletic office within 24 hours.
Direct student managers, assistants, and statisticians.
Determine discipline, delineate procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contact parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible.
Assist athletes in their college or advanced educational selection.
Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations.
Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records.
Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing.
Monitor equipment rooms and coaches' offices, authorize who may enter, issue or requisition equipment.
Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times.
Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Be responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment.
Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty.
Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings.
Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program. Promote the sport outside the school through news media, community recreation programs, or in any other feasible manner.
Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media.
Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans.
Present information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results. Perform other duties as assigned by the school Principal in keeping with Catawba County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes.
KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES
Must possess administrative and organizational skills in addition to a thorough knowledge of Catawba County Schools athletic policy and procedure as well as the North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes. Ability to communicate effectively with students, staff, parents, and the community.
MINIMUM TRAINING AND EXPERIENCE
As applicable, must meet requirements established by the North Carolina High School Athletic Association, North Carolina Department of Public Instruction, the Catawba County Board of Education, and school administration. Teacher license and employment as a teacher by the Catawba County Board of Education is strongly preferred. Previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport.
DISCLAIMER The preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications required of employees to this job.

Job.JobTitle: Head Volleyball Coach/Team Bus Driver (to include Cheerleading)
Job.JobDescription:
ATHLETIC COACH/Team Bus Driver (to include Cheerleading)
Under general supervision, assist the board in the fair and just discharge of its obligation to students and staff through the performance of the Head Coach role. Reports to the school principal and athletic director.
SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Catawba County Schools Board of Education and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the sports program.
Knowledge of existing school system, state, and conference regulations; implements them consistently and interprets them for staff.
Must hold or be willing to obtain a CDL with S and P endorsements (Bus License).
Understand the proper administrative line of command and refer all requests of grievances through proper channels. Awareness of the N. C. High School Athletic Association rules and regulations.
Maintians interscholastic athletics as an integral part of the total school program.
Understands the growth and development of students/athletes at the assigned school.
Responsible for grading athletes' performance,sic [sic] skills, and knowledge in sport involved as required by principal.
Follows state, regional, and district regulations governing the athletic program.
Models' [sic] sports-like behavior and maintains appropriate conduct towards players, officials, and spectators.
Acts as a team representative and promotes the sport by communicating with the news media, booster clubs, service clubs, and other organizations.
Follows established procedures in the event of an athlete's injury.
Participates in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations.
Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records.
Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing.
Monitor equipment rooms and coaches' offices, authorize who may enter, issue or requisition equipment.
Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times.
Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Be responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment.
Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty.
Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings.
Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program. Promote the sport outside the school through news media, community recreation programs, or in any other feasible manner.
Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media.
Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, Booster Club, parents, officials, volunteers, and fans.
Present information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments, and results. Perform other duties as assigned by the school Principal in keeping with Catawba County Board of Education Policy and North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes.
KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES
Must possess administrative and organizational skills in addition to a thorough knowledge of Catawba County Schools athletic policy and procedure as well as the North Carolina Administrative Code and Statutes. Ability to communicate effectively with students, staff, parents, and the community.
PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS
Must be able to physically perform the basic life operational functions of climbing, balancing, stooping, kneeling, couching [sic], crawling, reaching, standing, walking, pushing, pulling, lifting, fingering, grasping, talking, hearing, and repetitive motions.
Must be able to perform very heavy work exerting in excess of 100 pounds of force occasionally, and/or in excess of 50 pounds of force frequently, and/or in excess of 20 pounds of force constantly to lift objects.
Must possess the visual acuity to operate automotive equipment and inspect work for accuracy, and neatness.
MINIMUM EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE
High School Diploma required.
Minimum of 21 years of age required.
Commercial Driver's License (CDL) with S and P endorsements required.
* At least 2 year(s) of relevant experience preferred. * High School/Trade School degree preferred. * Citizenship, residency or work visa required
Job.Location: Challenger High School - Hickory, North Carolina
Job.Experience: At least 2 year(s) of relevant experience preferred.

Job.JobDescription: ATHLETIC COACH/Team Bus Driver (to include Cheerleading) Under general supervision, assist the board in the fair and just discharge of its obligation to students and staff through the performance of the Head Coach role. Reports to the school principal and athletic director. SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Thorough knowledge of all the Athletic Policies approved by the Catawba County Schools Board of Education and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. Knowledge of existing school system, state, and conference regulations; implements them consistently and interprets them for staff. Must hold or be willing to obtain a CDL with S and P endorsements (Bus License). Understand the proper administrative line of command and refer all requests of grievances through proper channels. Awareness of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by staff. Design conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program. Train and inform staff, encourage professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy. Delegate specific duty, supervise implementation, and at season’s end, analyze staff effectiveness and evaluate all assistants. Maintain discipline, adjust grievances, and work to increase morale and cooperation. Assist the Athletic Director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for tournament and special sport events. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adhere to scheduled facility times. Coordinate program with maintenance and school employees. Provide documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility. Provide training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Give constant attention to a student athlete’s grades and conduct. Presence at all practices and games. Provide assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant. Initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention, and emergencies. Complete and submit proper paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries to athletic office within 24 hours. Direct student managers, assistants, and statisticians. Determine discipline, delineate procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contact parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. Assist athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. Participate in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommend guidelines as to type, style, color, or technical specifications of equipment. Responsible for operating within budget appropriations. Accountable for all equipment and/or collection of fees for any lost or unreturned equipment. Arrange for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submit annual inventory and current records. Properly mark and identify all equipment before issuing or storing. Monitor equipment rooms and coaches’ offices, authorize who may enter, issue or requisition equipment. Permit the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Be responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. Secure all doors, lights, windows, and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty. Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. Organize parents, coaches, players, and guests for pre-season meetings.
Job.Experience: At least 2 year(s) of relevant experience preferred.

Job.JobDescription:
Job Description
POSITION PURPOSE: To assist the head coach in the organizing of the Men's Tennis program at the assigned school. To assist with coaching and supervising the Men's Tennis team (in season and out of season) as directed by the head coach and in accordance with the NCHSAA and OCS Board of Education policies. KNOWLEDGE OF POSITION: • Thorough knowledge of all aspects of the sport of Men's Tennis. • Thorough knowledge of the NCHSAA and OCS Board of Education policies related to athletics. • Thorough knowledge of state and federal requirements regarding physical examinations, parental consent, and eligibility for athletic participation • Thorough knowledge of training rules and any other unique regulations about the various sports in which athletes are engaged • Thorough knowledge of guidance and safeguards in place for the safety of athletes to address medical injuries and emergencies • Thorough knowledge of the process for initiating policies and procedures applicable to the athletic program
EDUCATION, TRAINING, AND EXPERIENCE • High School Diploma or GED • Additional coursework and/or training in a particular sport • Any equivalent combination of training and experience that provides the required knowledge, skills, and abilities
PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: • Associate's Degree or Bachelor's Degree from an accredited institution in Physical Education or related field • Minimum of three (3) years of coaching experience at the middle or high school level • NC Certification as Teacher, preferably in Physical Education
CERTIFICATION AND LICENSE REQUIREMENTS • Must possess a valid driver's license issued by the NC Department of Motor Vehicles
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Implement, interpret, and enforce consistently existing school system, state, and conference regulations ... (truncated due to length, but including key sections like duties, disclaimer, working conditions seen before) ... 33. Perform other duties as assigned by the Head Coach or Athletic Director
DISCLAIMER: The above statements are intended to describe the general purpose and responsibilities assigned to this position. They are not intended to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities, and skills that may be required of the employees assigned to this position. This description may be revised by the supervisor, with HR review and approval, at any time.
WORKING CONDITIONS PHYSICAL DEMANDS Depending on the sport, work may be considered light to heavy, requiring the exertion of up to 100 pounds of force. Position may require a lot of standing, walking, kneeling, squatting, pushing, pulling, and running.
WORK ENVIRONMENT Employees in this position are required to work in indoor and outdoor environments, with possible exposure to excessive heat and cold, and come into direct contact with OCS staff, students, and the public.
Job.Experience: 3 years

Job.JobDescription:
The Coach role at Polk County is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Polk County, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Polk County, the Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Coach at Polk County is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

Jamestown Public School District is seeking a passionate and experienced individual for an Head Girls Tennis Coach at Jamestown High School. Qualifications: Extensive knowledge of tennis fundamentals, strategies, and rules. Strong leadership skills Excellent communication abilities Please reach out to Mr. Jim Roaldson with any questions at 701-952-4005. Interested applicants may apply online at the below link: https://www.applitrack.com/ndseec/onlineapp/jobpostings/view.asp?internaltransferform.Url=&choosedistrict=true&applitrackclient=37936&category=Athletics%2FActivities Jamestown Public School District is an equal opportunity employer and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any kind. We provide equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants regardless of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or other status protected by law. External applicants are eligible for ND Veteran's Preference.
Job.Experience: Extensive knowledge of tennis fundamentals, strategies, and rules. Strong leadership skills Excellent communication abilities

Job.JobDescription:
The 6th Grade Boys Track Coach role at Jamestown Middle School emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the 6th Grade Boys Track Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Jamestown Middle School, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The 6th Grade Boys Track Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Jamestown Middle School at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The 6th Grade Boys Track Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The 6th Grade Boys Track Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The 6th Grade Boys Track Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Jamestown Middle School.

The 6th Grade Girls Track Coach at Jamestown Middle School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the 6th Grade Girls Track Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Jamestown Middle School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The 6th Grade Girls Track Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The 6th Grade Girls Track Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Jamestown Middle School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Jamestown Middle School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the 6th Grade Girls Track Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the 6th Grade Girls Track Coach role at Jamestown Middle School.

Job.JobTitle: Middle School Boys Assistant Track Coach
Job.JobDescription:
The Middle School Boys Assistant Track Coach role at Jamestown Middle School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Middle School Boys Assistant Track Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Jamestown Middle School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Middle School Boys Assistant Track Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Middle School Boys Assistant Track Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Middle School Boys Assistant Track Coach follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Jamestown Middle School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Middle School Boys Assistant Track Coach at Jamestown Middle School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

Job.JobTitle: Head Boys' Tennis Coach - Bennington High School
Job.JobDescription:
JOB DESCRIPTION JOB TITLE: Head Boys' Tennis Coach - Bennington High School REPORTS TO: Activities Director TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: Part-Time
I. ACCOUNTABILITY OBJECTIVES: To instruct athletes in fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. The student shall also receive instruction that will end to the formation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, self-confidence, resilience, and cooperation with teammates. Regular, dependable, in-person attendance is an essential function of the job.
II. POSITION CHARACTERISTICS: Recruited by: Athletic Director/Principal Wages: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Paid Vacation: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Paid Holidays: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Sick Leave: As determined by Negotiated Agreement Work Day: Hours to be determined by Activities Director/Principal Pay Day: As determined by Negotiated Agreement.
III. POSITION RELATIONSHIPS: Reports to Activities Director Coordinates with: Activities Director, Building Principal, Custodians, and Transportation Supervisor.
IV. POSITION RESPONSIBILITIES: Be responsible for the development and formulation of his/her program in grades 9-12 Oversee a systematic skill development program for all athletes in the program Recommend assistant coaches and coach's aides and officials to the Activities Director
Essential Functions The essential functions of the position include: 1. regular, dependable attendance on the job; 2. the ability to perform the identified tasks and to possess and utilize the identified knowledge, skills and abilities to perform the identified work activities; and, 3. the ability to perform the identified physical requirements as outlined in working environment.
While performing the duties of this job, the employee is required to lift and pull or push articles up to 50 pounds. He/she sits most of the time but must walk frequently and, at times, stand. The employee is required to reach with his/her hands and continuously to repeat the same hand, arm, or finger motion many times as in typing. Manual and finger dexterity are essential to the job. He/she is constantly working to meet deadlines with severe time restraints. He/she must see and read fine print such as typed or handwritten materials and have good depth perception (fine muscle control). The employee must, also, be able to hear conversation in a noisy environment and to communicate clearly and concisely through speech. -- The information contained in this job description is for compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and is not an exhaustive list of the duties performed for this position. Additional duties are performed by the individuals currently holding this position and additional duties may be assigned. EOE
Job.Benefits: Paid Vacation: As determined by Negotiated Agreement; Paid Holidays: As determined by Negotiated Agreement; Sick Leave: As determined by Negotiated Agreement

Job.JobDescription:
Varsity Head Swimming Coach @ LNE: Requires Teacher and/or coach who holds a Nebraska Department of Education Teacher or Administrative Certificate or a Nebraska Department of Education Special Services Coaching Permit. The position also requires CPR Certification and three required NFHS classes (Concussion Awareness, Heat Illness, Sudden Cardiac Arrest).
Certified teacher and/or coach that is responsible for the instruction of swimming/diving skills and techniques, team systems and strategies; responsible for providing a safe and competitive environment; developing safe and properly planned and supervised practices and contests; provides leadership for the overall program; is energetic, a good communicator and able to build relationships with student-athletes.
Responsible for inspecting and maintaining the inventory and equipment; reporting contest results; and must know, implement, and enforce national, state, and local athletic policies. Additional responsibilities would include evaluation of assistant coaches, ordering equipment, and organization of the entire swimming/diving program.
This position is subject to a veterans preference.
